Meaning of 'Qist': 'Qa'iman bil-qist' (Standing firmly for justice) is, in terms of composition, in our view, a state (Hal) from the pronoun in 'annahu'. The meaning is: Allah Almighty is One and Unique, He has no partner, all authority and disposal are in His hand alone, and He is using this authority and disposal exactly according to justice and equity. The meaning of 'Qist' is the same as what we express in common parlance with words like 'Haqq' (truth), 'Adl' (justice), and 'Insaf' (fairness). Its opposite is 'Zhulm' (oppression), 'Jawr' (injustice), and other words of this meaning. In every corner of the manifest and the hidden—thought, action, word, morals, character, manifestations, and forms—one point is that which is within the nature made by the Creator and Fashioner of everything and within His appointed limits; consider this the point of moderation or, in other words, the reference point of justice and equity. If in any corner a deviation even equal to a dot occurs from this point, this thing will be contrary to justice and equity. Depending on the changes in considerations and relationships, the expressions will change. In one circle, we will describe deviation from it as oppression and injustice; in one corner, as ugliness and deformity; similarly, in one aspect, we will describe this moderation as truth and justice; in one place, as beauty and grace; but the original reality will be the same everywhere. That is: a thing shifted from its original natural place, so corruption appeared; and if it became joined to its joint, then order manifested. The Creator of the universe, since He is the Creator and Master of this world, desires its order, not its corruption. He has so fitted the joints of His creative system that there is no room for any loophole to be produced; and if through some miracle of His power a loophole seems to be appearing anywhere, His designing hands suddenly manifest to correct it so that no disorder occurs in the balance upon which this machinery is established. This same love for balance of His is also for that circle of our life in which He has given us a limited type of freedom. When we use our choice wrongly to create corruption in some corner of our morals and actions, He does give us respite, but this respite is only to a specific limit; His love for justice does not tolerate that He leaves us free to follow our desires and consequently leaves His creation to be ravaged and trampled; rather, He seizes upon this respite and corrects the corruption we created anew, because He is 'Standing firmly for justice.' It is for this 'Standing firmly for justice' that He has placed the law of the recompense of deeds; it is for this that He started the series of sending prophets and Sharias; it is for this that He ensured that when corruption arises in the Sharia through alterations and innovations, renewers and reformers risk their lives for its reform and renewal; it is for this sake that He made the rise and fall of nations subordinate to their moral rise and fall; and then above all, for the perfect manifestation of this justice and equity, He has appointed such a Day in which His balance of justice will be installed and it will weigh and tell whose deed is full in the scale and whose is not, and then reward and punishment will occur accordingly. Here, this point should also be noted: in a single verse, the declaration of Tawheed is repeated twice, and with both, a reference to two different attributes of Allah Almighty is made. First, He stated that there is no deity except Allah, He is 'Standing firmly for justice'; then He stated that there is no deity except Him, He is the Mighty and Wise. In this style, there is a severe warning for the addressees, the People of the Book. The meaning is: the testimony of God, the angels, and all bearers of knowledge is that there is no 'Ilah' (deity) except God, and that 'Ilah' is not detached from the affairs of the world such that He would leave people like unbridled camels in the pasture of their desires, they wandering around and He taking no notice despite being the Ever-Living and Sustainer; rather, He will surely establish His system of justice and equity despite your desires, and no one will be able to catch His hand. Then He stated: why would He not do so when He is both the One without partner and the Mighty and Wise? Both His honor and His wisdom demand that He do so; if He did not, it would mean that He is either helpless and devoid of the sense of truth, or He is a 'player' who made the world merely a play and sport. Obviously, such a concept cannot even be imagined regarding the magnificent Being of God.

Faith in 'Qist' is among the Important Pillars of Faith: Believing in Allah Almighty's attribute of 'Standing firmly for justice' is among the extremely important components of faith in Allah, and in the reality of Islam, it has such a role that Islam effectively consists of it. This importance demands that a few points of the teacher-imam regarding it be recorded here so that those who wish to reflect on the wisdom of religion can benefit from them. According to Maulana (RA), the importance of this attribute is from the following aspects:

Four Aspects of the Importance of Faith in 'Qist': 1. 'Iman' (faith) is from 'Amn' (security/peace), meaning that trust and conviction are inherent in its nature. From this, it follows that for faith, it is indispensable that a person has firm conviction in the existence of Allah. But this thing cannot be achieved until it is trusted that reason is originally made for guidance, not for misleading. Meaning: it is accepted that reason, according to its nature, is a balance of 'Qist' within the human. Then this thing necessitates another result: that the Creator (Fatir) has established nature upon the principles of truth and justice. This is because Allah Almighty is, in every way, Justice and Equity, a lover of justice and equity, and the Establisher of it. All these results are rationally mandatory—in fact, among the self-evident truths. From this, it was known that the proof of something being 'the truth' is not even possible until the Creator of nature is accepted as Truth and Justice. From this, the being of all His actions as truth and correctness will be proven. Just as this thing is mandatory rationally, its proof is also provided by the moral nature. The detail is that Allah Almighty has included goodness in nature and deposited in hearts the desire to accept and respect it. In such a state, how is it possible for us to like goodness ourselves and not declare God as a lover of goodness? How can we be satisfied with the correctness and rectitude of this love for goodness of ours if our heart is not satisfied with the Creator's own love for goodness? We want to please Him by doing good only because we hold the satisfaction that He likes goodness. Describing Him with good attributes is also on this basis: that we have full trust in the correctness of our nature regarding the liking of these attributes. 2. Second, the original spirit of faith is the love of God. We believe in a Deity whom we love, from whom we hope, and whose pleasure we seek. This thing is not even possible until we are certain that He is pure from every taint of oppression and injustice. He will bestow His reward only upon those who obey Him and give punishment only to those who deserve it. Loving some oppressive and unjust master is entirely against human nature. 3. Third, the demand to believe in Allah Almighty that emerges in nature from reflecting on Allah Almighty's rewards and favors is based on gratitude (Shukr). This gratitude becomes mandatory when we accept that this is the right of the Bestower (Mun'im) and the requirement of His reward. This is the secret why in the Quran, Shirk is declared 'oppression' (Zhulm) and faith is declared 'gratitude' (Shukr). On this same principle, the foundation of the entitlement to all rights is placed on the obligation of justice. This is a self-evident reality of Sharia and law. For this reason, the base and foundation of every Sharia is 'Qist'. 4. Fourth, the fruit of faith is the obedience of Allah, and the fruit of obedience is Allah Almighty's pleasure. Allah Almighty has established this relationship between actions and their effects in every corner through His creation and design and His order and command, and He has guided us toward this reality in various ways; and since we have full trust in these results of actions, we obey Him while relying on His promise. If we did not have faith in the fact that Allah Almighty cannot go against His promise, the foundation of all actions would collapse, and then all reliance would remain on one of two things: either like the Christians on false intercession—whose entire reliance is on Jesus (PBUH), whom they made a deity, worship him, and love him more than God; or like the Jews on complete wandering and lack of foresight. They left their boat to the direction of the wind; due to their arrogance and envy, they were not pleased with God's decision—as if in their view, there is no code at all with Allah Almighty to distinguish between the good and the bad. To escape this misguidance, it is necessary to have full conviction that Allah Almighty is 'Standing firmly for justice,' His every command is justice, and His every promise is true. As He stated: 'And the word of your Lord has been fulfilled in truth and in justice.'

Anyone who reflects on these four aspects will have the reality well-clarified to them that faith in 'Qist' is among the extremely important pillars of faith, and upon it are based the most fundamental issues of beliefs, morals, and Sharias.

"Ad-Din" refers to the true religion, i.e., that religion which Allah Almighty revealed for the guidance of His servants. The definite article 'Al' on it is like that on "Al-Kitab." We explained this at the start of the exegesis of Surah Al-Baqarah. "Al-'Ilm" refers to the knowledge of truth which was revealed by Allah Almighty to clarify the truth and remove difference. The meaning is that since Allah is the Establisher of justice and equity, He gave a religion of justice and equity to tell servants the correct way of living. This very religion is implemented in the entire creative system of this universe. Human nature was created upon this very religion. This is the religion He revealed to all prophets and messengers from the beginning. Apart from this, He gave no other religion to anyone. But the Jews and Christians, due to mutual difference and enmity and stubbornness, created many differences in it and set up their separate religions under the names of Judaism and Christianity. This difference of theirs was not based on any ignorance but occurred despite the truth becoming clear, purely out of the mischief of the soul, mutual enmity, and the pursuit of their respective innovations. In this way, they received Allah's great blessing and wasted it. Since Allah is the Ever-Living, Sustainer, and Establisher of justice, He revealed this system of justice and equity—i.e., Islam—anew in a fresh and perfect form so that people find the straight path of guidance and achieve success in both the world and the Hereafter. If even now they persist in the same way they adopted before and continue to deny God's verses, then let them remember that Allah Almighty is very swift in settling accounts. Meaning: they should not consider this respite they have received as very long; rather, in the words of Prophet John (Yahya), they should understand that the axe is placed at the roots of the trees. This subject has already appeared in Surah Al-Baqarah, and we discussed it in detail there. We quote the verse; those seeking details should see its exegesis in that place. It was stated: 'Mankind was [once] one community [ummataw-wahidah]; then Allah sent the prophets as bringers of good tidings and warners and sent down with them the Scripture in truth to judge between the people concerning that in which they differed. And none differed over the Scripture except those who were given it - after the clear proofs came to them - out of jealous animosity [baghyan] among themselves. And Allah guided those who believed to the truth concerning that over which they had differed, by His permission. And Allah guides whom He wills to a straight path' (Al-Baqarah: 213).

"aslamtu wajhiya lillah: I have surrendered my face to Allah" is an expression for surrendering one's entire self to Allah. The face is the most noble and honorable part of a human's self. When the most noble and honorable part is surrendered, then effectively everything is surrendered. This is an expression similar to how we say "bowing the head" to denote someone's obedience. In this expression, the highest degree of humility, neediness, and entrustment is found. The context proves that here this style was used primarily to convey the meaning of entering Islam, but simultaneously, the original spirit of Islam was also clarified from it so that these claimants of religiosity—who had rolled up their sleeves for debate and dispute in opposition to Islam—might be warned as to what thing they are showing this force against. 

Meaning of 'Ummi': 'Ummi' refers to one unacquainted with formal and book-based education and learning. The word 'Ummiyyin' is used as a title for the Ishmaelite Arabs. The reason is that these people remained established upon their nomadic simplicity, unacquainted with formal and official education and writing, and thus, in contrast to the Children of Israel who were bearers of the Book, 'Ummi-ness' was a distinguishing mark for them. It is possible that the start of its use for the Arabs was from the People of the Book themselves. This is because the nomadism and 'Ummi-ness' of Prophet Ishmael and his descendants is mentioned in the Torah as well. But it is perfectly clear that there was no element of contempt for the Arabs in the use of this word. Consequently, the Quran used this word for the Arabs merely to distinguish them from the People of the Book. From this same aspect, the title 'Nabi-e-Ummi' (the Unlettered Prophet) was used for the Holy Prophet (PBUH). This also contains an allusion to the prophecies of the Torah. The Arabs themselves used this word for themselves, which is extremely clear proof that they found no element of contempt in it for themselves. Some Hadiths show that the Holy Prophet (PBUH) also used this word for his nation. For example, the Hadith in which it was stated: 'nahnu ummatun ummiyyatun...' (we are an unlettered nation). In some places, if this word is used by way of contempt, then its meaning there is merely literal, not terminological. For example: 'wa minhum ummiyyuna la ya'lamunal-kitaba...' (And among them are unlettered ones who do not know the Scripture...). This refers to the illiterate masses of the Jews. 

"a-aslamtum: Do you also submit?" This style consists of both a threat and an invitation; in fact, to an extent, it also expresses disgust. Meaning: if you also want to enter Islam, then do so; we no longer want to waste our time in polemics with you; we have chosen our path and are not ready to delay our destination. The meaning of the verse is: when the Jews and Christians distorted Allah's religion—Islam—into the form of Judaism and Christianity, they did so deliberately; and now, the polemics and debates they have triggered in support of Judaism and Christianity and in opposition to Islam are also deliberate. They well know what the truth is and what the status of the religion you are calling to is. For this reason, nothing is gained by wasting time in debate and dispute with them. Tell them and the unlettered Arabs as well: I and my companions have surrendered ourselves to Allah and placed the collar of Islam around our necks. If you too are ready for this gamble, then 'Bismillah' (in the name of Allah), move forward; and if you are not ready, then leave our path; we no longer want to waste our time behind you. After this, the Prophet (PBUH) was consoled: if they adopt the path of Islam, [good]; and if they do not adopt this path but want to persist in their follies, then leave them to their state; your responsibility was only to deliver the truth, and you have fulfilled that responsibility; now you are absolved of your duty. Now their decision is in Allah's hand; He is watching all the circumstances and dealings of His servants, and He will deal with each one as He finds them deserving.

In 'yaqtuluna-n-nabiyyina bi-ghayri haqqin', through the words "unjustly" (bi-ghayri haqqin), on one hand, the gravity of the Jews' crime is being expressed—because unjust killing is itself a grave crime, and if this unjust killing is of a prophet, then its gravity is beyond words. On the other hand, the greatness of the Truth (Haqq) is being expressed: that Truth is the most superior thing, to the extent that even prophets fall under it. Our Holy Prophet (PBUH) expressed this reality in various ways. 'alladhina yamuruna bil-qist' refers to those reformers and renewers who arose from among the common people and who struggled for the reform of the Jews' and Christians' alterations and innovations and to bring their lives anew under the system of truth and justice given by God. The Jews and Christians dealt with these people in the same way they dealt with God's prophets. They used all their weapons to oppose them, and they did not hesitate to kill whomever they could overpower. 

At the end of the previous verse, it was stated that Allah is the Monitor of His servants' state, and He will grant whichever of guidance or misguidance He finds someone deserving of; now, its implications are unfolding. The meaning is: regarding those whose habit has always been to deny Allah's verses, who have been killing Allah's prophets, and who have been killing those who rose among them with the call of reform, renewal, and truth and justice—how is it possible that their temperament would change today? The system of truth and justice which they always distorted, and whose establishers on its original foundations they persecuted and killed—how will they tolerate that same system of justice and its callers today? When the successors are walking in the very footsteps of their predecessors, how can you expect from the children of the killers of prophets and reformers and the distorters of the Torah and Gospel that they will tolerate you and the Book you present with a cool heart? The instances in the Gospel where Sayyidina Jesus expressed despair regarding the faith and salvation of the children of the killers of prophets are also exactly on this point. The Quran has said the exact same thing here in its own style: that these people are not worthy of the glad tidings of salvation and success by adopting the path of faith and Islam. Just give them the 'glad tidings' of a painful punishment.

The Reality of 'Habt-e-'Amal' (Wasting of Deeds): 'Habt-e-'amal' means the efforts and labors becoming worthless. Obviously, all those acts of religiosity which, in the words of Sayyidina Jesus, were equivalent to straining the gnat and swallowing the camel—the question of them being fruitful in the Hereafter does not even arise. As for the efforts and strategies they were spending in opposition to the Quran and Islam—or in other words, Allah Almighty's system of justice and equity—this verse prophesied their worthlessness as well, because in this verse, the wasting of their deeds in both the world and the Hereafter was mentioned, and simultaneously it was stated that in saving them from this humiliation and failure, no helper will be able to provide them any help. Whether these helpers be spiritual or material, history bears witness that the Quran's prophecy was fulfilled letter by letter.

Summary of Verses 23 to 27: First, astonishment is expressed at the state of these deniers of the Quran: being People of the Book, the expectation from them was that when they found the Book anew in its perfect form—a part of which had been given to them but they had lost through their wretchedness—they would value it and, in its light, resolve all their differences and adopt the path of justice and truth once more; but they adopted the way of aversion according to their habit. Following this, a very important cause of this aversion of theirs is traced: due to considering themselves a chosen Ummah, they have invented the false creed that they are protected from the punishment of Hell in every form—firstly, they will not be cast into Hell at all, and even if they were, it would only be for a cursory period to suffer some punishment before attaining salvation. This creed is a self-invented one of theirs which has no connection to reality, but this very self-deception has made them entirely heedless of their religion, even though one day every single bit will be accounted for. On that day, their eyes will open to see that all their deeds are before them, and God's balance has shown neither any partiality nor any deficiency toward them. Following this is the announcement that these Jews and Christians are now dismissed from the position of leadership, and this trust of the Divine Sharia is being handed over to this Ummah which God found worthy of this trust. "God alone is the Owner of Sovereignty; He snatches it from whom He wills and grants it to whom He wills; honor and disgrace are all in His hand; entering the night into the day and bringing the living out of the dead is His work." This point is taught to the Prophet (PBUH) and through him to his companions in the style of a prayer rather than a statement, which contains a hint that those upon whose shoulders this heavy burden is now being placed should consider it God's trust and seek only His support in carrying it. In this light, recite the following verses.

The Nature of the Address 'Alam tara': We have already written on the address 'Alam tara' (Have you not seen) in Surah Al-Baqarah. It is not correct to take it in the singular meaning in every place; its address is also to a group, as if every individual of the group is addressed personally. Furthermore, it is not merely a simple address but also carries the meaning of expressing astonishment. "Nasiban minal-kitab" (a portion of the Book) refers to the Torah and the Gospel, etc., and "Kitab Allah" (the Book of Allah) refers to the Holy Quran. Just as the relationship between previous heavenly religions and the Islamic Sharia is that of a part to a whole, similarly the relationship between other heavenly scriptures and the Quran is that of a part to a whole. Allah's Sharia was granted stage by stage according to the gradual evolution of the human mind and human society. Until the human was worthy of the perfect Sharia and the perfect Book, they were not given the perfect Sharia and perfect Book, but were given a Book according to their circumstances and their needs; but this Book was originally a part of that perfect Sharia and that perfect Book which was already appointed for them in God's scheme. The teaching given by the prophets of the Children of Israel is not something separate from the teaching of the Seal of the Prophets, Muhammad the Messenger of Allah (PBUH), but was that portion of this teaching which was suitable for their era and their circumstances; similarly, the Torah and the Gospel are not something separate from the Holy Quran but were those pages of this perfect scripture which were revealed for the nations before the final Ummah. In this way, all heavenly Books hold the status of different parts and different chapters of the single Divine Book; originally and naturally, there is perfect harmony and consistency among them. If mixtures and alterations had not occurred in the Torah and Gospel, no difference would be seen between their teaching and the Quran's teaching except for summary vs. detail and beginning vs. completion. Nonetheless, even today a person of vision can easily estimate that all scriptures are lights of the same niche of knowledge and gnosis and the leaves and fruits of the same Tuba tree. Due to this mutual consistency, it was not difficult for those who know and believe in the Torah and Gospel to recognize the Quran, provided they had not tied the bandages of prejudice and stubbornness over their eyes. The Book whose initial chapters they had read, with whose style, method, and temperament they were acquainted, whose initial reflection and sketch of instructions and teachings they had seen, of whose prophecies they were aware and for whose referent they were waiting—how is it possible that when that beloved Book manifested in the guise of its original beauty and perfection, they would not recognize it? Then how astonishing it is that the People of the Book turned away from it and, knowing it, became strangers to it. "li-yahkuma baynahum" (to judge between them): this describes that purpose of the Quran which should have been very dear to the People of the Book if they were appreciators of Allah's Sharia. It has been stated above that Allah Almighty revealed the Torah and Gospel for their guidance, but they created many differences in them; therefore, Allah Almighty revealed the Holy Quran as a 'Furqan' (Criterion) so that it could decide all these differences and clarify the original truth again. This same point is repeated here: that they are being called toward this Book not because there is any benefit for the one calling, but it is entirely for their own benefit. They are being called so that the differences they created in God's Sharia are removed and they benefit again from the guidance they had lost. "thumma yatawalla fariqum-minhum wa hum mu'ridhun": 'thumma' (then) here is in the sense of expressing wonder, and "fariqum-minhum" (a group of them) does not aim to show that only a small group of the People of the Book is turning away from this Divine Book—because the People of the Book as a community were guilty of this crime, with only a few emerging from among them who became believers, as is clear from other places in the Quran—rather, the original force is on the word 'minhum' (from among them). Meaning: the expression of wonder is at the fact that this turning-away group is of those People of the Book who were most deserving of being the first to believe in it, yet they became the ones to excel in Kufr instead of excelling in faith. Finally, through "wa hum mu'ridhun" (and they are averse), light was shed on the original communal temperament of the People of the Book: that this aversion of theirs from the truth is not a new thing but has always been their way. Within this phrase, there is an element of severe reproach for the People of the Book and an element of consolation and comfort for the Prophet (PBUH). The meaning is: regarding those whose second nature and national temperament has become aversion from the truth—if the truth does not enter their heart, the fault is not yours or the truth's, but of the corruption of their temperament. Where can a leech attach to a stone! Those acquainted with the history of the Children of Israel know that from Prophet Moses (PBUH) to Sayyidina Jesus (PBUH), every single prophet without exception lamented their temperament. If there were no fear of lengthiness, we would present examples of this from the scriptures of the prophets of the Children of Israel.

This subject has already passed in Surah Al-Baqarah. We clarified every aspect of it there. It was stated there: 'And they say, "Never will the Fire touch us except for [a few] numbered days." Say, "Have you taken a covenant with Allah? For Allah will never break His covenant. Or do you say about Allah that which you do not know?" Yes, whoever earns an evil deed and his sin has encompassed him - those are the companions of the Fire; they will abide eternally therein' (Al-Baqarah: 80-81). In Al-Baqarah, this verse appears within the context of the false hopes ('amaniyy') of the Children of Israel for which no authority existed in their religion. Their scholars had invented them from their own minds and made them a part of their Sharia, and being pleasing to the heart, these innovations had become so established in the hearts of their masses that all their reliance now rested on these false hopes. Among others, they had this self-invented creed that since the Children of Israel are a chosen Ummah, their deeds—whatever they may be—do not result in eternal punishment of the Fire; firstly, they will not be cast into Hell at all, and even if they were, they would be released after suffering a little punishment for their sins. Obviously, after inventing this creed, no need remained for them to think about or understand the original responsibilities of religion; then why would they turn toward this Quran which wanted to take them out of this 'fools' paradise' and stand them face-to-face before those responsibilities that actually fell upon them according to their religion? The Quran clarified that their creed is 'iftira' 'alallah'—meaning a slander and a fabrication against Allah Almighty. He has nowhere given the Children of Israel this license that they may do whatever they wish and reach Paradise after just a few days of punishment. Simultaneously, He stated that in reality, it is these types of self-invented innovations and superstitions in which they have become afflicted that have caused them to be deceived regarding their religion. Now their religion has remained a collection of a few liked desires and a few captivating dreams, in which there are countless rights for them with God, each greater than the last, but it imposes no responsibility upon them except for a few rituals.

Meaning: they are sitting with these hopes and seeing these delicious dreams regarding the Hereafter, but what will happen when We gather them to see the states and results of a 'Day of Decision' (Yawm al-Fasl) which is an inevitable reality of this universe and regarding whose occurrence there is no room for doubt? In our view, the 'Lam' here can also be in the sense of 'fi' (in/during), because it also contains the meaning of 'zarfiyyah' (container of time/place). And according to the rules of the Arabic language, there is also room to assume a 'mudhaf' (possessive noun) is omitted after the preposition. We have clarified the verse by keeping this second aspect in view. One characteristic of that Day described is that there is no doubt in its coming; the second is that on that Day, every soul will be paid in full for its earning, and no deficiency will be made for anyone. The meaning is: now let these People of the Book think for themselves what reality they have to face after waking up from this pleasant dream they are enjoying today while afflicted with a self-invented creed.

A Great Glad Tiding for the Muslim Ummah in the Style of a Prayer. This is a prayer taught to the Holy Prophet (PBUH) and through him to the entire Ummah, which consists of the following matters:

1. The first thing is the great glad tiding for this Ummah hidden within it. The detail of this summary is that this prayer is secretly hinting that despite the stubbornness and envy of the People of the Book, the position of leadership and sovereignty upon which the Children of Israel have been exalted until now is now being transferred to the Ishmaelites, and no opposition from the Children of Israel can change this decision of God. God is the Owner of His kingdom; He grants the kingdom to whom He wills and snatches it from whom He wills; He gives honor to whom He wills and disgrace to whom He wills; He is the Owner of all treasures of goodness. The Children of Israel, who have been thinking that they alone are the heirs and inheritors of all goodness and that no one else—especially the Ishmaelites—has any share in this goodness, find the board of this false thought of theirs overturned by this prayer. It contains the hint that now the Master of the universe has decided to snatch the keys of this treasure of goodness from the covenant-breakers and traitors and hand them over to those worthy of this trust. 2. Along with this glad tiding, it contains a warning (indhar) for the Children of Israel, and this warning is in reality the natural result of the aforementioned glad tiding: when sovereignty is obtained by the Ishmaelites, the Children of Israel will necessarily be deprived of this sovereignty; when the Ishmaelites are exalted with honor, disgrace has necessarily been destined for the Children of Israel. By mentioning both 'appointment and dismissal' (nasb o 'azl), 'honor and disgrace', and 'life and death', the Quran announced the decision of destiny for both its opponents and its supporters. 3. It contains a great advice for this Ummah: now that this trust is being transferred to you, do not think like the Jews that this is a miracle of your personal entitlement or the fruit of your lineage; rather, it is entirely God's favor, of which you will remain worthy as long as you fulfill its right and continue to pray to God regarding the carrying of its responsibilities.

From this detail, it became clear that this verse is in reality the announcement of the Jews' dismissal and the glad tiding of the Muslim Ummah's appointment, but the point was said in the style of a prayer rather than [a statement of] goodness and glad tiding. There are two reasons for this. One is that until the time of these verses' revelation, the circumstances were still behind the veil, and regarding what is behind the veil, this style is most suitable: that the Ummah prays for it. Second, it contains a hint toward the reality that the Ummah should not accept this glad tiding with pride and arrogance, but with humility, self-abasement, a sense of servitude, and prayer, because all authority is in Allah's hand alone; whoever is deprived is deprived by God's command, and whoever receives receives from God's hand.

Some other extremely eloquent examples of glad tidings appearing in a prayerful style also exist in the Quran. For example, just before the command for migration (Hijrah), the Prophet (PBUH) was taught this prayer: 'And say, "My Lord, cause me to enter a sound entrance and cause me to exit a sound exit and grant me from Yourself a supporting authority." And say, "Truth has come, and falsehood has departed. Indeed is falsehood, [by nature], ever-bound to depart."' (Al-Isra: 80-81). This prayer contains the goodness of the migration and simultaneously the glad tiding that both your exit from Makkah and your entry into the Abode of Migration will be with honor, and you will be granted a special escort and a special decree of victory from Allah Almighty for protection. In fact, it contains a subtle glad tiding that the arrangement for your dignified entry has already been made before your exit. This is hinted at by the fact that in the prayer, the mention of 'entering' is placed before the mention of 'exiting'; in fact, if you reflect more, the point also emerges from the verse that the migration is in reality the preface to victory and the preamble to the dominance of truth. But all these points were said in the style of a prayer instead of the style of a statement and glad tiding, and the wisdom in this is the same one we hinted at above.

Testimony of the Creative System: In 'tuliju-l-layla...', there is a reference to the testimony of the creative system of this universe regarding the point mentioned in the previous verse. The meaning is: who else in the world could have the authority of dismissal and appointment and honor and disgrace except the God who enters the night into the day and makes the day manifest after the night, and who brings the living out of the dead and the dead out of the living. This 'entering the day into the night and entering the night into the day' is an extremely beautiful simile for the coming and going of night after day and day after night. It seems both are pursuing each other with full activity; sometimes the night enters into the day, sometimes the day hides within the night; this cycle is running with perfect continuity. This simile is described in various styles in the Quran. Similarly, the signs of life appearing from death and death from life also continue to manifest in every corner—in material things and in spiritual things. This is also a subtle critique of the situation manifesting from the death of the Children of Israel and the life of the Ishmaelites. The plant that Prophet Abraham had planted in the lush and green land of Palestine had now dried up and, as Prophet John (Yahya) said, the axe was placed at its root. In contrast, the plant he had planted in the dry and barren land of Arabia, which had been lying withered, was now sprouting buds and, as Sayyidina Jesus (PBUH) said, was going to become a massive tree taking a whole world into the refuge of its shade.

'wa tarzuqu may-tasha'u bi-ghayri hisab'. 'Rizq' here is an expression for favor and reward, not in the limited meaning of daily bread. 'bi-ghayri hisab' (without account) here consists of two meanings. One, in the meaning of quantity—meaning He blesses whom He wills with immeasurable favor and reward that has no limit or end. As stated: 'Indeed, the patient will be given their reward without account [bi-ghayri hisab].' Second, in the meaning of 'beyond expectation'—as stated: 'And He will provide for him from where he does not expect [la yahtasib]' (At-Talaq).

Summary of Verses 28 to 32: From the previous verses, it became clear that the status of the People of the Book is now that of a ruined house whose collapse has been destined; therefore, in the following verses, those weak Muslims afflicted with hypocrisy who held an inclination toward the People of the Book—especially the Jews—were warned: maintaining 'Muwalaat' (intimacy/alliance) with them now is like being the gatekeeper of a ruined house, and the result is nothing other than that when that house falls, those who went under its walls in search of shade will also be crushed beneath it. Following this, their hypocrisy was warned against: if disbelief and love for the people of disbelief are hidden in their hearts, they should remember that nothing is hidden from God. He knows everything; a day will come when everyone's entire hidden and manifest will come before them; on that day, God's justice will manifest and everyone will taste it. Since Allah Almighty is extremely Kind to His servants, He is informing them of that Day beforehand. Then, the correct demand of faith and love for God was clarified: it is not permissible for those who claim faith in Allah and His love to maintain 'Muwalaat' with the opponents of Allah and His Messenger; rather, the correct method for them is to follow Allah's Messenger. Those who do so, God too will love them; this is the path to becoming beloved to God. Those who adopt a way contrary to this are in reality disbelievers, and Allah Almighty does not love the disbelievers. In this light, observe the following verses.

In this verse, "believers" refers to the wavering Muslims and "disbelievers" refers to the Jews. Although the word "believers" (mu'minun) is apparently general, it refers specifically to those Muslims who had not yet become entirely single-minded but, partly due to their personal interests and partly because of being unsatisfied regarding the future of Islam (as passed above), held an inclination toward the Jews; and the Jews used to make them tools in the conspiracies they plotted against Islam and the Muslims, and these people would become their tools. Addressing them, it was stated: 'Muwalaat' and friendship with the Jews now is both the gatekeeping of a ruined house and an act contrary to the claim of faith and Islam. "Disbelievers" (kafirin) here refers to the People of the Book, especially the Jews, as the clarification of their Kufr passed in verse 21. "Wali" means designer, supporter, companion, friend, and helper to whom one turns at the time of need and whom one supports with a spirit of zeal and protection. It stated: it is not permissible for Muslims to make disbelievers their 'Walis', but this is accompanied by the qualifier "min dunil-mu'minin" (instead of the believers)—meaning only that type of 'Muwalaat' with disbelievers is unlawful which is in opposition to the Muslims or against their interests and benefits. The right and interest of Islam and the Muslims is prior to all other rights and interests; therefore, it is not permissible for any group of Muslims to establish a relationship of 'Muwalaat' with any group of disbelievers contrary to the interests and benefits of Islam and the Muslims. This qualifier clarified that there is no prohibition of that goodness, justice, and excellence toward non-belligerent disbelievers which Islam has instructed regarding all humanity. Muslims can also make friendly political and economic treaties with non-Muslim nations and governments provided they are not "min dunil-mu'minin." We will discuss this issue in detail later at a suitable place.

The Correct Meaning of 'illa an tattaqu...': 'illa an tattaqu minhum tuqah' (except that you guard against them as they should be guarded against). 'Tuqah', just as it was used as an absolute object (maf'ul mutlaq) in verse 102 of this Surah ('ittaqu-llaha haqqa tuqatih'), is an absolute object here too, through which the emphasis of the verb is manifest. The meaning is: those Muslims who maintain a relationship of 'Muwalaat' with disbelievers against Islam and the Muslims have no connection with Allah. Such people, as stated elsewhere—'And whoever among you takes them as allies, then indeed he is [one] of them'—are included among those with whom they maintain 'Muwalaat'; friendship cannot be established with both Allah and the enemies of Allah simultaneously. For friendship with Allah, it is necessary that you guard your mantle from those who are enemies of Allah, His religion, and His loyal servants. This phrase is effectively an exception from 'falaysa minallahi fi shay''—meaning only those are exempt from this negation who guard against the anti-Islamic 'Muwalaat' of these disbelievers in the way it should be guarded against. Those who have derived the permissibility of 'Taqiyyah' (dissimulation) from this verse have ignored the lexicon, Quranic parallels, and context; but after the correct interpretation has been clarified, the need for this refutation no longer remains.

At this point, this verse of the Holy Quran should also be kept in view: 'You will not find a people who believe in Allah and the Last Day having affection for those who oppose Allah and His Messenger...' (Al-Mujadilah: 22).

A Specific Aspect of Warning for the Hypocrites: In 'wa yuhadh-dhirukumullahu nafsah' (And Allah warns you of Himself), there is a specific aspect of warning for the hypocrites. It is: do not be deceived by Allah's generosity and ignore the other aspects of His Being. If He overlooks mischiefs, ignores conspiracies, and does not take immediate notice of machinations, it does not mean these crimes are not crimes in His sight or that He cannot seize upon these crimes; rather, the only reason is that He gives servants respite to the final limit. This respite is nonetheless a respite that must end one day. After that, His justice will manifest, and this justice is also an aspect of His Being. If it has not manifested yet, let no one think that it will not manifest at all. There is delay in God's works, but not injustice; when this aspect of His Being comes forward, it will be revealed to everyone that there is no one more powerful, more impartial, and no greater Avenger and Crusher than Him. Allah Almighty has warned of this very aspect of His Being here, and it will be clear later that He warned repeatedly. In another place it was stated: 'ma gharraka bi-rabbikal-karim' (O man, what has deceived you concerning your Lord, the Generous?)—this too contains a hint toward this reality. The same weak and hypocritical Muslims warned here are also mentioned further on in this Surah. From that, light is shed on some hidden corners of this verse. Observe: 'O you who have believed, do not take as intimates [bitanah] those other than yourselves, for they will not spare you [any] ruin. They wish you would have hardship. Hatred has already appeared from their mouths, and what their breasts conceal is greater. We have certainly made clear to you the signs, if you will use reason. Here you are loving them while they do not love you, and you believe in all of the Scripture. And when they meet you, they say, "We believe." But when they are alone, they bite their fingertips at you in rage. Say, "Die in your rage. Indeed, Allah is Knowing of that within the breasts." If good touches you, it distresses them; but if misfortune strikes you, they rejoice at it. And if you are patient and fear Allah, their plot will not harm you at all. Indeed, Allah is encompassing of what they do' (Al Imran: 118-120).

In "ma fi sudurikum" (what is in your breasts), there is a hint toward that hypocrisy and friendship with the people of Kufr which these people held in their hearts. It stated: hide it or reveal it, nothing is hidden from God. He is aware not only of the secrets of your hearts but also of whatever is in the heavens and the earth and whatever is happening, and He is powerful over everything. Despite this knowledge and power, if He is giving respite, it is because He has appointed a specific Day for reward and punishment in which everyone's goodness and evil will come before them, and will come with such results that those who were deceived by this respite—considering that Day so distant that they became indifferent to any worry or arrangement for it—will wish that a vast distance of time stood between them and these results of their deeds.

After 'ma 'amilat min su'', the word 'muhdharan' (presented) is omitted; since it was expressed in the first segment, it was omitted in the second to avoid repetition. In 'baynaha wa baynahu', the referent of the first pronoun is the soul (nafs), and the second is the 'presented evil' (su' muhdhar). 'wa yuhadh-dhirukumullahu nafsahu wallahu ra'ufum-bil-'ibad': We have discussed the word 'Ra'uf' elsewhere, noting that the aspect of warding off evil is dominant in it. Meaning: since Allah Almighty is extremely Kind to His servants and He wants to save them from the evil results of their deeds, He is repeatedly warning them of His Being so that they do not fall into any misunderstanding due to His respite; He does indeed give respite, but when He seizes, His grasp is also very severe.

Exegesis of Verses 31-32: The Correct Path for the Believers: These wavering Muslims are taught the correct attitude they must adopt as true Muslims. It stated: if you claim to have love for Allah, then this love cannot coexist with the love of those who are enemies of Allah, His Book, and His religion; rather, the path to it is to follow Allah's Messenger. If you follow the Messenger, then this is the path to loving Allah, and its reward is that Allah too will love you and will forgive the errors and weaknesses you have committed until now. Allah is Forgiving and Merciful. Following this, in extremely clear words and a threatening tone, this announcement was made through the tongue of the Prophet (PBUH): warn them to straightforwardly obey Allah and His Messenger, and if they turn away from this, then let them remember that they too are included among those disbelievers with whom they have friendship, and along with that, let them remember that Allah does not love the disbelievers.

In these two verses, some points are specifically to be noted. One is that the tone of both is different. In the first verse there is compassion, and in the second there is warning—rather, threat. As if 'harshness and softness are together in the same degree.' Second, the original spirit of faith is the love of Allah, and for this love, the condition is that no such love is gathered with it which is its opposite. Third, the only path to loving Allah is following the Messenger; the paths carved out apart from this are all innovations and misguidance. Fourth, the path to being beloved by God is also following the Messenger. If someone's life deviates from the Prophet's Sunnah yet they are afflicted with the presumption that they are God's beloved, or others consider them God's beloved, this is entirely wrong. Fifth, the minimum demand of religion is the obedience of Allah and the Messenger. If someone adopts aversion from fulfilling this demand, they are counted among the deniers of religion. Allah Almighty does not hold love for such people.

Subject of Verses 33 to 44: The introduction to the Surah ended at verse 32. Now, the refutation of the Christians' innovations begins, which holds the status of the primary subject in this Surah. This too begins with a preamble. First, there is a reference to the chain of guidance and rectitude which Allah Almighty established for the guidance of this world. In this context, Adam, Noah, the family of Abraham, and the family of 'Imran are mentioned—that Allah Almighty chose them for the leadership and guidance of the world. Within this group, the mention of the family of 'Imran is effectively the preamble to the mention of Sayyidina Jesus, because Maryam (Mary) is the 'light of the eye' of this blessed family, and it was from the womb of this Maryam that the blessed birth of Sayyidina Jesus occurred. The purpose of referencing this lineage from Adam to the family of Abraham and the family of 'Imran is so that regarding Sayyidina Jesus and his respected mother Maryam, the point becomes clear to the Christians: what is proven from their own recognized history is not that Sayyidina Jesus or his mother are some superhuman beings, but that they belong to this same 'Golden Chain' of guidance and rectitude which Allah Almighty chose for the guidance of the world. This blessed family certainly attained the honor of being chosen for guidance and instruction from Allah Almighty, but this choice was for the worship of Allah and the call to His worship; just as there are other chosen servants of Allah in this chain, similarly Sayyidina Jesus (PBUH) is also a chosen servant of Allah. Then where does the justification arise for giving him and his mother the status of divinity? Further on, the events of the early life of Maryam (PBUH) are referenced: how before her birth, her mother made a vow for the child in her womb; then when, contrary to expectation, a girl was born to her, what kind of distress she faced; then how Allah Almighty removed this distress of hers; how Zakariyya (Zachariah) took her into his training; and how Allah Almighty blessed her with His acceptance, to the extent that Zakariyya himself—a possessor of blessings—was so influenced by her spiritual blessings that he prayed for righteous offspring for himself too. The purpose of referencing this story of Maryam (PBUH) is to clarify to the Christians that this is the story of a pure-natured, submissive, and obedient servant-girl of Allah Almighty, and not—according to their presumption—God forbid, the mother of God! Following this is the mention of the acceptance of Zakariyya's prayer, despite the fact that he himself had entered the final stage of old age and his wife was barren; yet Allah Almighty gave him the glad tidings of the birth of Yahya (John), and he was born according to this glad tiding. This is a hint that a birth contrary to habit is a sign of Allah Almighty's power; it does not mean that whoever's birth is contrary to the general code of causes should be made a god or an avatar. If the Christians made Jesus (PBUH) a god on the proof of his birth being contrary to habit, then this proof exists in favor of Yahya too!

In this light, now read this preamble to the refutation of Christianity. It is stated:

Exegesis of Verses 33-34: The Family of Sayyidina Jesus (PBUH): Adam, Noah, and Abraham (PBUT) are the pillars and dignitaries of the chain of Prophethood and Messengership. When they are mentioned, it is as if the entire blessed chain of Prophethood is mentioned. Along with the mention of Prophet Abraham (PBUH), the mention of his family gathered both branches that sprouted from him: meaning the branch of Prophet Isaac, whose final Prophet is Sayyidina Jesus (PBUH), and the branch of Prophet Ishmael (PBUH) in which the Seal of the Prophets, Muhammad the Messenger of Allah (PBUH), was sent. After the family of Abraham, the mention of the family of 'Imran here holds a special importance. This is the mention of that blessed family in which the blessed birth of Maryam (PBUH) occurred. 'Imran bin Matan is the name of Maryam's (PBUH) respected father. He is the maternal grandfather of Sayyidina Jesus (PBUH). The purpose of mentioning this entire lineage is to show that Sayyidina Jesus (PBUH) is also a link in this same blessed chain; his mother, his maternal grandfather, and his other ancestors are all known; all these families are linked and joined to each other and are each other's descendants. The meaning is: then what is the sense in elevating a person raised from this family to the station of divinity?

'wallahu sami'un 'alim' (And Allah is Hearing and Knowing) contains a hint that those whom Allah Almighty chose for Prophethood and Messengership—this choice was based entirely on Hearing and Knowing; He chose for this position those whom He found worthy of it. This thing depends entirely on eligibility, capability, and Allah Almighty's wisdom and benefit; the personal honor of any family has no role in it, as presumed by those afflicted with the pride of noble descent.

The Early Story of Maryam (PBUH): After clarifying the lineage of the family of 'Imran in the previous verse, the birth of Maryam (PBUH) is now mentioned: when she was still in her mother's womb, her mother, the wife of 'Imran, had made this vow that she would dedicate the child to be born for Allah Almighty. The purpose of dedicating a child for Allah among the Children of Israel was that they would be specified for the service of the temple. "Muharraran" means 'freed/dedicated.' Meaning: upon growing up, this child will have no responsibility for home or earning; their entire life will be dedicated solely to the service of Bait al-Maqdis. Further on, it comes that Maryam's mother expected the birth of a boy, but a girl was born. This thing caused her hesitation because there was no custom of taking girls and women for the service of the Temple; but Allah Almighty accepted the vow of Maryam's mother, and she was admitted into the Temple. The purpose of describing this early story of Maryam (PBUH) and the subsequent circumstances is to clarify: how can a servant-girl of Allah—whose life was dedicated to God and His Temple and to Allah's worship and obedience even before being born, and remained dedicated to it from birth until her final breath—be made, God forbid, the mother of God instead of a servant of God? What loss of reason!

'inni wadha'tuha untha' (I have delivered a female) yields the hint that Maryam's mother, as passed above, expected the birth of a son and had made the vow on that expectation; but the birth, contrary to expectation, was of a girl. Upon this, she expressed her hesitation before her Lord: that I have delivered this girl, and nonetheless the child whom I had presumed for Your vow was, according to my thought, a boy; this girl cannot be his substitute, but even so, if You accept this humble vow, it will be Your favor upon favor. 'wallahu a'lamu bima wadha'at' (And Allah knew best what she delivered)—this is an incidental phrase (jumlah mu'taridhah) from Allah Almighty in the middle of Maryam's mother's statement. Maryam's mother saying 'inni wadha'tuha untha' was indicative of a feeling of inferiority regarding the newborn, and she felt her gift before Allah Almighty was very humble. Allah Almighty, out of His extreme kindness and mercy, clarified that while Maryam's mother was considering Maryam a very humble thing due to being a girl, Allah well knew what a great and blessed being had manifested from her womb in the form of a girl! 'wa laysadh-dhakaru kal-untha' (and the male is not like the female)—this is part of Maryam's mother's statement, and its meaning is what we described above: meaning, where is that boy who was in the mind and where is this girl who came into existence; she cannot be his substitute, yet if You accept, it is Your kindness. 'wa inni u'idhuha bika wa dhurriyyataha minash-shaytanir-rajim' (and I seek refuge for her in You and her descendants from Satan the expelled)—this prayer from Maryam's mother for Maryam and her descendants was a natural thing. The purpose of this mention is: the girl for whom her mother asks this prayer and regarding whom she feels such a degree of inferiority while presenting her as a vow to God—the Christians later present her as the Mother of God. The Quran's objective in presenting this entire story is to bring forward the original nature of the events and show people how simple and straightforward events have been turned into a myth.

The Spiritual Excellence and Perfection of Maryam (PBUH): 'fataqabbalaha...' (So her Lord accepted her...): Meaning, contrary to the feeling Maryam's mother had due to her being a girl, Allah Almighty blessed her with His beautiful acceptance; all her rational, moral, and spiritual capabilities flourished well. And it was also a special favor of Allah Almighty that the responsibility of her maintenance and training was taken by Zakariyya, who was Maryam's maternal uncle-in-law and, in that era of Bait al-Maqdis, the 'High Priest' (Kahin-e-A'zam) in Israelite terminology. 'kullama dakhala 'alayha zakariyyal-mihraba wajada 'indaha rizqa' (Every time Zakariyya entered upon her in the sanctuary, he found with her provision). 'Mihrab' refers either to that part of the temple specified for women's worship and I'tikaf, or a specific corner and chamber specified for Maryam. In Bait al-Maqdis, such chambers and corners were built for worshippers. 'kullama dakhala 'alayha zakariyyal-mihraba' yields two points simultaneously. One, that Zakariyya used to visit Maryam frequently to look after her; second, that Maryam spent all her time in the sanctuary in remembrance and worship. 'wajada 'indaha rizqa' expresses Maryam's extraordinary spiritual perfection: even a person of perfection like Zakariyya, when visiting her, would sense the fragrances of her spiritual perfection, to the extent that one day, by way of wonder and praise, he even asked: "O Maryam! From where do these things come to you?"

"Rizq" refers to Wisdom and Gnosis: "Rizq" here refers to wisdom and gnosis (Hikmat and Ma'rifat). The Quran has used this word for revelation and guidance in more than one place. This expression also exists in the Torah and Gospel. Sayyidina Jesus's famous statement is: "Man does not live by bread alone, but by every word that comes from the Lord." In the next verse, it comes that Zakariyya was so influenced by Maryam's words of knowledge and gnosis that he, in old age and despite his wife's barrenness, prayed for similar righteous offspring for himself too. Obviously, a person of gnosis like Zakariyya could not be so influenced by a 'provision' of apples and grapes that he would start praying for children upon seeing that miracle. Such things do not hold any special rank or status with people of perfection. A person of perfection like Zakariyya could only be influenced by such a spiritual provision that would also inflame his own spiritual appetite, seeing which he too would be filled with admiration, and which would produce within him the desire: if only an heir to this perfection would arise from his lineage too. 'anna laki hadha' (From where is this for you?) is not for the purpose of inquiry and research but by way of wonder and praise. When someone's perfection is very great relative to their age and far beyond the speaker's presumption and thought, such wonder is natural. This wonder is a style of expressing praise. It also expresses Zakariyya's humility and appreciation: how generously he is praising the capabilities of a girl under his training whose age is still nothing. Maryam's response—'huwa min 'indillah' (It is from Allah)—is also a witness to her maturity of reason in that young age: that she attributed all this to Allah's favor and kindness, and did not attribute it to her own asceticism and discipline. 'innallaha yarzuqu may-yasha'u bi-ghayri hisab' (Indeed, Allah provides for whom He wills without account)—in our view, this sentence is not part of Maryam's response but is an expression of Allah Almighty's praise for Maryam and His infinite favor. We have explained the meaning of 'bi-ghayri hisab' above.

'Hunalika' (There/At that time) expresses that Zakariyya was influenced by Maryam's amazing wisdom and gnosis to such an extent that the desire for children, which was buried within him, suddenly flared up: if only Allah Almighty would bless him too with such an heir of wisdom and gnosis. Consequently, he prayed to Allah Almighty for this. Through the words 'min ladunka' (from Yourself), it is manifest that due to his old age and his wife's barrenness, he was finding the outward circumstances unhelpful, but he held the hope in Allah Almighty that if He wills, through His favor and power, even an old man's desire can be fulfilled and even a barren old woman's womb can become green. Causes/means are merely the veil of the manifest; the original thing is God's power and His favor.

The word 'Mala'ikah' (angels) here and wherever it appears in this specific context is in the plural form. In our view, the reason is that Zakariyya heard the call of an 'unseen caller' (Hatif-e-Ghaybi); he did not recognize the angel with specificity. Due to this ambiguity, the Quran mentioned 'angels' instead of a specific angel, which yields the point that the voice Zakariyya heard was celestial, but simultaneously expresses that it was merely an unseen voice that fell upon his ears. 'wa huwa qa'imuy-yushalli fil-mihrab' (while he was standing in prayer in the sanctuary): meaning, he heard this voice of the unseen caller while he was standing in prayer inside the chamber; this was a clue that this voice must be celestial, because the state of prayer is most suitable for the proximity and connection of an angel. Another hint from this is that the most suitable time for prayer, supplication, and secret conversation with one's Lord is when the servant is in prayer. It is from this that life and heat are produced in prayer, and this prayer establishes the link of our life directly with our Creator and Master. The emphasis on prayer in all difficulties and issues given to the Prophet (PBUH) in the Quran also yields the point that the key to solving life's difficulties lies within it. The details of the prayers of the Holy Prophet (PBUH) and the Companions found in Hadiths also give the impression that their prayers were in reality prayers of secret conversation with God; all their supplications and pleas were in the prostrations and standing of prayer, and when they returned from their prayers, they returned with their laps and pockets filled. For them, prayer was a necessity of life just as a ghat (watering place) is a necessity for the thirsty. This glory no longer remains within our prayers. Now prayers have remained merely a matter of ritual; their connection with life has vanished. Now we perform prayers that are lifeless and spiritless, and we ask long supplications after finishing the prayers, even though the original time for asking is in the prayers when the servant is in the presence of their Lord. 'annallaha yubash-shiruka bi-Yahya...' (that Allah gives you glad tidings of Yahya...): this is the same Yahya whose name appears as John in the Gospels. From the Gospels' description, it is known that he was born only six months before Jesus. Along with the glad tiding of his birth, three characteristics of his were described. One, that he will be 'musaddiqam-bi-kalimatim-minallah'—meaning he will confirm a Word from Allah and give glad tidings of it. 'Kalimatim-minallah' (a Word from Allah) refers to Sayyidina Jesus (PBUH). Consequently, further on in verse 45, his mention occurs explicitly with this title: 'O Maryam, indeed Allah gives you glad tidings of a Word from Him whose name will be the Messiah, Jesus, son of Mary' (Al Imran: 45). The reason for calling Sayyidina Jesus (PBUH) 'Kalimatullah' is that his birth occurred contrary to the general code of causes, through Allah Almighty's word 'Kun' (Be). Through the 'indefiniteness' (tankir) of 'Kalimah', the point is also clarified that among Allah Almighty's countless words, Sayyidina Jesus (PBUH) is also one word. Just as countless things in this universe have manifested from the mere word 'Kun', similarly Sayyidina Jesus (PBUH) also manifested from this word. This refutes a claim of the Christians: they say that 'Kalimatullah' is only the Messiah, and then they prove the creed of his divinity from it. The 'Ba' in 'bi-kalimatin' is a clue that the word 'tasdiq' (confirmation) here also consists of the meaning of 'glad tidings.' Meaning: Yahya will both confirm Jesus and give glad tidings of him. It is proven from the Gospels that he performed both these duties. The mission he clarified for his own life was precisely that he had come to clear the way for the one coming. Consequently, he risked his life for the completion of this mission. His life was the embodiment of confirming Sayyidina Jesus (PBUH). His birth too, understand, was in one aspect contrary to habit just like the birth of Jesus. In asceticism, trust in God, and celibacy too, he was exactly the 'first sketch' of the one coming after him; and as for the heralding, he heralded Sayyidina Jesus (PBUH) with such glory that the wilderness and mountains echoed with it. Yahya's (PBUH) second characteristic is that he will be a "Sayyid" (leader/master). 'Sayyid' means a leader. A prophet is a leader by his nature, his call, and his mission. He calls people as a 'Da'i' (caller), wakes people as a 'Munzir' (warner), and guides people as a 'Hadi' (guide) and 'Murshid' (mentor). For this task, he is armed by Providence with all requirements and weapons. His breast is overflowing with compassion and kindness for the creation; there is immense power and majesty in his speech; there is awe in his voice and his style; his radiant forehead bears witness to his greatness and truthfulness. Even if he wears a garment of wool and subsists on wild honey and locusts, kings tremble from his awe and majesty. He rebukes them for the truth just as he rebukes others. It comes in the Gospels regarding both Yahya and Jesus that they spoke like one 'possessing authority.' Obviously, this glory of possessing authority in their speech was a manifestation of this official leadership of theirs. In his custody is also a flock from Providence whose shepherding is entrusted to him, and no difference occurs in his customary status from the fact of whether the flock obeyed him or not. If he performed his duty, he fulfilled the right of leadership, and this is what is required of him. This word completely refutes the presumption that Yahya was some monk and his life was separate from the creation; he was undoubtedly an ascetic regarding his own self, but every moment of his life was dedicated to the heralding of that repentance for which he was commissioned, and he had his head cut off in this path. (It is in the Gospel that Yahya wore a garment of wool and subsisted on wild honey and locusts, but he severely rebuked the ruler of the time for an indecency of his).

Yahya's (PBUH) third characteristic is that he will be "Hasur". 'Hasur' is on the 'Fa'ul' pattern from 'Hasr', the literal meaning of which would be 'one who keeps himself enclosed/restrained.' From here, its usage became for a person who is detached from the pleasures of the world and keeps themselves in perfect control. Although this self-control is among the characteristics of that leadership mentioned above—because only one who can keep themselves in control will be able to keep the creation in control—the lives of both Yahya and Jesus (PBUT) were entirely dervish-like; they did not even benefit from those pleasures of life which in general circumstances are not considered worldliness in any degree. But it seems the circumstances of these gentlemen were specific. In their era, the love of the world had so overcome the Jews that to turn their direction, they had to present a completely ascetic and dervish-like model of life. This is a form of 'treatment by opposites' which, like physical matters, must be adopted in spiritual and moral matters in specific circumstances. The objective from this would be that this Ummah gradually becomes capable of adopting that point of moderation which was finally to come before them in Allah's final religion; but the Christians turned this asceticism of theirs into monasticism and set up an entire system of monasticism in later eras.

The fourth characteristic is that he will be a Prophet: 'nabiyyam-minas-salihin'. The meaning of 'Nabi' is clear. However, the clarification 'minas-salihin' (from among the righteous) alongside it aims to state their category: that despite all attributes and perfections, they were from the category of the righteous; it is not that they attained some station of divinity. Whereas, besides perfections and virtues, they also hold a relationship of kinship with Sayyidina Jesus (PBUH), and their birth is also very similar to the birth of Sayyidina Jesus (PBUH)—in fact, it is even proven from the Gospels that he baptized Sayyidina Jesus (PBUH), and Sayyidina Jesus said regarding him that among those born of women, none is greater than John.

This question is not of the nature of wonder, doubt, or denial, but is an extremely beautiful and eloquent style of seeking confirmation. By describing the obstacles in the manifestation of this glad tiding before him, Zakariyya desired to have it clarified that despite these obstacles, the form of this glad tiding's manifestation would be thus. 'kadhalika-llahu yaf'alu ma yasha'' (Thus Allah does what He wills): this matter will be just so. Meaning: Allah's will is just so—that Yahya's birth occurs to an old father and a barren mother. The original thing is Allah Almighty's will alone. Causes/means are merely the veil of the manifest. If Allah wills, springs of water can flow from within a stone and bubbles can rise from the breast of the desert. A similar question and answer is also reported in the story of Prophet Abraham (PBUH) in the Quran; its nature is exactly the same.

Zakariyya (PBUH) had heard these things from an unseen caller, and had heard them in a good hour and good circumstances; therefore, he did presume that this glad tiding was from Allah. But he was an extremely humble, pious, and cautious servant; therefore, in a corner of the heart, there was also the hesitation: perhaps this was the echo of his own heart's dome; perhaps the hidden desires of the soul had some role in it which Satan tried to exploit. For this reason, he requested his Lord: "O Lord, appoint for me a sign so that I may be satisfied that this glad tiding is from You, and there is no deception of the soul or Satan in it." Allah Almighty accepted this request of his and stated: the sign for you is that you will not be able to speak to anyone for three days and nights except by gesture; however, you will be able to perform the remembrance of Allah Almighty and His glorification, so during this time, remember Allah much and be engaged in the glorification of your Lord evening and morning. Obviously, the overcoming of a person by such a state for a specific duration—that they cannot speak any worldly talk but can perform Tasbih and Tahlil—cannot be the result of some satanic intervention; it can only be a miracle of Rahmani (Divine) intervention. If this point were produced from some satanic effect, its result should have manifested exactly the opposite: meaning, the person could speak their worldly talk but doing 'Allah Allah' would be heavy upon them. If this state was imposed upon Zakariyya involuntarily, it was certainly a definite sign that the glad tiding of a son he received was from Allah, and satanic deception had no role in it. The Quran here also incidentally refuted the point stated in the Gospel of Luke: that this state Zakariyya faced was as a punishment for his crime of not believing the angel's word and asking for a sign.

Those not well-acquainted with the Quran's style of expression might face the doubt: the verse mentions that you will not be able to speak to anyone except by gesture for three days, but it does not explicitly state that you will be able to perform Zikr and Tasbih. The answer to this doubt is that in place of that explicit statement, the instruction for Tasbih and Tahlil has been placed in the verse. Obviously, this instruction was given precisely because he could do this work. If that explicit statement were also present with this instruction, it would be a useless lengthiness not befitting the Quran's eloquence. 'bil-'ashiyyi wal-ibkar' (in the evening and the morning) and other such styles, as we clarified elsewhere, are proof of the meaning of 'encompassing.' Just as we say: remember Allah night and day, morning and evening.

Exegesis of Verses 42-43: 'Istifa'' means to sift and select. In Quranic terminology, its meaning is Allah Almighty's selection of one of His servants for a specific special task. Allah Almighty had chosen Maryam for the manifestation of one of His great signs. This sign was a very great Divine trust that was to be entrusted to her, and simultaneously a great trial. This thing required that Allah Almighty provide her with special training for carrying this burden of trust so that she could be capable of facing the circumstances in the upcoming stages. This training is described here as 'Tathir' (purification). Then, regarding this 'Istifa'', He clarified that this 'Istifa'' was no ordinary selection but was "over the women of the worlds." When 'ala' (over) follows 'Istifa'', the meaning of preference and excellence is also produced within it. The meaning is: Allah Almighty chose her from among all the women of the world to entrust His great trust. This is an honor in which Maryam has no partner or peer. For the preparations for this burden of trust, the unseen caller instructed her: 'uqnuti li-rabbiki...' (be devoutly obedient to your Lord...). We clarified the meaning of 'Qunut' elsewhere: to bow toward Allah Almighty with full neediness and full self-abasement. The best expression of this neediness and self-abasement occurs in prayer. For this reason, 'wasjudi warka'i' (and prostrate and bow) was effectively the detail of the summary of 'uqnuti'. A nuance of eloquence is also to be noted here: prayer is mentioned here through its important components. This style, wherever adopted by the Quran, expresses the absorption and immersion in prayer, its constancy, and the anxiety and restlessness for it. God willing, we will clarify this nuance while interpreting the verse 'tarahum rukka'an sujjada'. The qualifier "ma'ar-raki'in" (with those who bow) clarifies the importance of congregational prayer too, and this is also a picture of the prayer whose blessing Maryam (PBUH) possessed. Since she was in I'tikaf in the Temple, she possessed the blessings of congregational prayers alongside the prayers of privacy.

A Digression (Iltifat): This single verse holds the nature of a digression addressing the Prophet (PBUH) in the middle of the speech. Addressing you, it was stated that these are news of the 'Ghayb' (unseen)—meaning they are outside your knowledge and information, because neither are all these points present in the Torah and Gospel, nor were you personally present at the time these events occurred; then, your presenting these events with such accuracy and truthfulness that the eyes of the People of the Book also open—how was this possible without Allah blessing you with the position of Messengership and distinguishing you with the honor of revelation? This is a very great proof of your Prophethood and Messengership over the People of the Book. 

The fact is that in the Gospels, this part of the People of the Book's history is almost missing; only some disjointed points are found in Luke regarding Yahya (PBUH) and some hints regarding Maryam. And the most regrettable thing is that the mention of Maryam that does exist seems like the mention of an ordinary woman; the limit is that from some places in the Gospel, it is even inferred that Sayyidina Jesus did not respect her in the way a mother should be respected. Whatever rank the Christians might have given Maryam as a creed, the truth is that her original and true honor has been highlighted only by the Quran. We will discuss this issue in detail later at suitable places. 

'Aqlam' refers to the arrows of casting lots. The use of arrows for gambling is Haram in the Sharia, but there is no evil in the use of arrows for casting lots. In the case of equal rights, the method of casting lots is perfectly permissible for settling a dispute. A question also arises here: was this method of casting lots adopted only regarding the guardianship of Maryam, or was this method also prevalent for other servants of the Temple under training? In our view, both points are possible. It is possible that the decision for the guardianship of all incoming servants was made through this method, and it is also possible that Maryam's case was of a specific delicacy due to being a girl, which is why it was decided by lot. A lot is also interpreted as a sign from the unseen. In the Temple, the method of lots was prevalent for the distribution of the duties of its servants. It is mentioned in Luke that on the day Zakariyya received the glad tiding of a son, the decision for the service he was commissioned for had been made through lots. In 'wa ma kunta ladayhim idh yakhtashimun' (and you were not with them when they disputed), the dispute mentioned does not seem related only to the guardianship of Maryam (PBUH); if that were the case, there was no need to mention it separately, but it would have come under the first segment. In our opinion, this dispute must have occurred among the servants of the Temple on the question of whether a girl can be included in the category of Temple servants. we hinted above that its tradition was not well-known in the history of the Temple. For this reason, this question could be a cause of dispute. Anyway, it is also not far-fetched that mere 'competition in goodness' (tanafus fil-khayr) was the cause of this dispute, because the guardianship of such a girl who was being dedicated for the service of the Temple and whose acceptance was manifest from day one was a very great blessing from which none of the Temple servants would like to be deprived. 

Subject of Verses 45 to 63: now that original point is coming which is in reality the 'column' ('amud) of the Surah. We hinted in the introduction that the address in this Surah is to the Christians, and the objective is to express the reality of the matter regarding Sayyidina Jesus (PBUH) to them. Above, the lineage of the family of 'Imran, the birth of Maryam and her mother's vow regarding her, Zakariyya's prayer for a son, and the events of Yahya's birth have all been described as the preamble and introduction to the mention of Sayyidina Jesus (PBUH).
