Now, further on, it is described that just as the angel gave Zakariyya the glad tiding of a son, the angel also gave Maryam the glad tiding that from her womb, through Allah's word 'Kun', a son would be born whose name would be the Messiah, Jesus, son of Mary. Just as it was stated regarding Yahya that he would be a leader, disciplined, a prophet, and righteous; then just as Zakariyya expressed wonder at this glad tiding due to his old age and his wife's barrenness, similarly Maryam too expressed wonder: how would she have a child when no man had touched her. The angel gave the same response to Maryam that he gave to Zakariyya: the original thing is Allah Almighty's command; when He decides to do a task, He commands it to be, and it is. Consequently, through this word 'Kun', He will create the Messiah, Jesus, son of Mary, and will teach him the Book and Wisdom and will send him as His Messenger to the Children of Israel. 

Following this, in three verses, is that initial message from the tongue of Jesus which he gave to the Children of Israel as proof of his Messengership and expression of his purpose. Then, in two verses, is the statement that when the scholars and jurists of the Children of Israel disappointed him with their stubbornness and obstinacy, he left them and made those poor people his confidants and companions who believed in him, and he invited them to roll up their sleeves for the support and aid of God's religion; consequently, these people became his companions and he sent them on the mission of preaching the religion. After that, in four verses, the reaction of the leaders, jurists, and Pharisees of the Children of Israel to this final effort of Sayyidina Jesus (PBUH) was mentioned, along with a reference to how Allah Almighty helped Sayyidina Jesus (PBUH) and his companions and promised to help them in the future. After that, five verses are of digression (Iltifat) in which, addressing the Prophet (PBUH), it was clarified: the original reality regarding Sayyidina Jesus (PBUH) is this which has been described. If after this clarification the Christians still argue with you, then tell them: come, let us perform 'Mubahala' (mutual imprecation); if they avoid even this, then understand that these are confirmed corrupters; refer their matter to Allah. Now, in this light, observe the following verses. It is stated:

The repetition of 'Idh' (When) is proof that the point being said here is separate from what was said above. First, the emphasis on Maryam to engage in prayer and worship occurred, then after some time, the angel came to Maryam with this glad tiding. The meaning of 'Kalimah' and the benefit of its being indefinite (nakarah) passed above. Here, respecting the occasion, one point is worthy of attention. It is that the original glad tiding was that Maryam would have a son without the meeting of a man, merely by Allah Almighty's order and command; but the point was being said to Maryam, who was both a virgin and the embodiment of modesty and shame. For this reason, with extreme brevity—rather, ambiguity—only the glad tiding of a 'Word' was given. However, further on, from the mention of Jesus's name and attributes, it became clear what was meant by the 'Word.' "Messiah" is Sayyidina Jesus's (PBUH) title. The rule for a title is to bring it before the name. There has been a tradition in the Children of Israel that for the one who was to be a prophet, his predecessor prophet would rub a type of holy oil on his head to make him his successor. When the series of kingship started along with Prophethood among them, this same tradition of anointing (mas-h) was also adopted for kings. The prophet of the time would rub holy oil on the head of the future king. This would clarify that he is both the future king and God's chosen one; it is known from the Torah that Talut and Sayyidina David were commissioned by Prophet Samuel in this way. Regarding the Messiah, it is proven from the Gospels that Yahya baptized him, but there is no mention of rubbing oil. It is possible the reason is that he was the 'born Messiah.' The description of his appearance in Sahih Bukhari shows that the state of his head was as if oil were dripping from it. It is possible that due to this characteristic of his, he was granted the title of Messiah. In the Gospel, the terms "God's Messiah" were also used for him. Through the word "Wajih" (distinguished/honored), there is a hint toward that glory of leadership whose mention passed above in the description of Yahya. Luke's tradition shows that at the age of 12, the Messiah taught in the Temple for the first time, but despite this young age, the wisdom and gnosis of his teaching, the eloquence and majesty of his speech, and the greatness and glory of his tone were such that the jurists and Pharisees, the high priest and the entire staff of the Temple, were left breathless. In a state of wonder, they went around asking each other: who is this who speaks with such majesty that it seems authority has been given to him from heaven? When he started preaching in the settlements of Judea, a stir was created from one end to the other. The creation pounced upon him. A state of consternation was upon the jurists and Pharisees; to corner him and reduce his popularity among the masses, they asked him various types of questions, but Sayyidina Jesus gave them such crushing answers in two-two words that they did not dare to open their tongues again. In a few days, such a clamor of his distinction arose that the masses began calling him the King of Israel and singing songs of his kingship, to the extent that even the Roman rulers—Herod and Pilate—faced this issue as an extremely important one; but they too, despite all their power and majesty, were overawed by Sayyidina Jesus's greatness and truthfulness and his immense popularity. The second aspect of this 'Wajahat' (distinction) is: although Sayyidina Jesus was born without a father—and for any child born without a father, in general circumstances, no honor or distinction can even be imagined—Sayyidina Jesus, since he was born from Allah Almighty's word 'Kun', the miraculous effect of this manifested such that from day one, he possessed that distinction in the eyes of the creation which no one else possessed in that era. He remained surrounded by his mortal enemies throughout his life, but no one had the audacity to taunt him from this aspect. If a group of Jews did commit this audacity, they did so in later eras; in his blessed era, no one could have this type of audacity. This glad tiding of his distinction was given to Maryam along with the glad tiding of his birth so that she would have no hesitation from the aspect that due to the child being born without a father, there would be any effect on the child's or her own distinction. The third aspect is that this refutes all those absurdities mentioned in the Gospels: that the Jews, God forbid, slapped Sayyidina Jesus, mocked him, cursed him, and spat on his face. Most of these absurdities, as we will clarify later, are wrong. The enemies of God's messengers do commit the audacity of insulting and belittling them, and they do receive respite from Allah Almighty to a certain limit in this regard, but this respite is only to a specific limit; when a nation commits the audacity to exceed that limit, Allah Almighty takes His messenger into His protection and that wretched nation is ruined. We will clarify this Sunnah of Allah further on. 

Sayyidina Jesus is the Son of Mary: By calling Jesus 'Ibn Maryam' (Son of Mary) in this verse, the Quran has ended every room for discussion for those who want to alter the extremely clear texts of the Quran through very weak interpretations. If Jesus were the son of some father, then after all, what was the obstacle for the Quran in attributing him to his father instead of calling him the Messiah, son of Mary? The Quran too could have said Messiah, son of Joseph, but it did not say so. After all, why?

Sayyidina Jesus speaking in the cradle was a miracle from Allah Almighty for the expression of Maryam's chastity. The glad tiding of this miracle was given to Maryam along with the glad tiding of the child's birth so that she would remain satisfied: if Allah Almighty has made her the medium for the manifestation of His great sign, then He has also made such an arrangement to save her honor from the bad-mouthing of enemies that no room will remain for any slander. Allah Almighty is extremely Kind to His servants; how was it possible that He would make one of His believing and devout servant-girls the target of the slanders of all creation and not open some tongue in her defense that would shut everyone's tongues? "Kahl" means middle-aged/mature. From the current Gospels, it seems that Jesus departed the world long before becoming middle-aged, but from this verse of the Quran, it is clearly known that Maryam was incidentally given the glad tiding of Jesus (PBUH) reaching maturity too. According to the Sunnah of Allah regarding messengers, only this point seems correct. Some hints in the Gospel also support this. For example, in John 8:57: "Then said the Jews unto him, Thou art not yet fifty years old, and hast thou seen Abraham?" Obviously, this point can only be said addressing someone who is approaching fifty years. Referencing his speech in maturity alongside his speech in the cradle yields the hint that his cradle-talk will not be like that of children but will contain the wisdom of mature years. This is because this speech will be from Allah. Finally, by saying 'wa minas-salihin' (and from the righteous), as we hinted above, it was clarified that he would be from the category of the righteous—meaning despite all these perfections and attributes, it is not that he attained some status of divinity; he would merely be among the righteous servants of Allah.

The clarification of the important components of this verse passed under verse 40. However, in this, the 'Word' through which Jesus was born has also been clarified: 'idha qadha amran fa-innama yaqulu lahu kun fa-yakun' (When He decrees a matter, He only says to it, "Be," and it is).

The words Torah and Gospel are here as an exegesis of 'Book' and 'Wisdom.' The meaning is that Allah Almighty will teach him both these things. As far as the Book and Sharia are concerned, Sayyidina Jesus was a follower and caller to the Sharia of Moses; he did not come with some Sharia separate from the Torah. He himself announced this reality repeatedly with great force and emphasis. His clarifications exist in the Gospels. However, he unveiled the spirit and wisdom of that Sharia in an extremely miraculous manner, and the Gospels are in reality the collection of these wisdoms of his. The Jews had turned the Torah into a collection of entirely spiritless commands and lifeless rituals; for this reason, their Sharia, being entirely empty of life, had remained merely a burden for them. Jesus produced life within it through his teaching of wisdom, but the Jews did not value it.

Before "Rasulan" (as a Messenger), a verb is omitted. Meaning: 'yab'athuhu rasulan' (He will send him as a messenger). Sayyidina Jesus was not merely a prophet like Yahya, but just as Moses was sent as a Messenger to Pharaoh and his people, similarly he was sent as a Messenger to the Children of Israel. 

Difference between Nabi and Rasul: There is a difference between a Rasul (Messenger) and a Nabi (Prophet). A Rasul is sent to a nation as God's court. Through him, the decision for that nation is necessarily made. If it believes, it attains salvation; and if it persists in its Kufr and tries to harm its Prophet, it is ruined. Toward this reality, Yahya pointed through various styles. For example: "I indeed baptize you with water... but he that cometh after me... shall baptize you with the Holy Ghost, and with fire"; or: "And now also the axe is laid unto the root of the trees"; or: "Whose fan is in his hand, and he will throughly purge his floor, and gather his wheat into the garner; but he will burn up the chaff with unquenchable fire." The full detail of this will come elsewhere. From this, the specificity of Sayyidina Jesus's (PBUH) Messengership for the Children of Israel also became clear. Sayyidina Jesus's own announcement is also this. When he sent his disciples on the mission of preaching the religion, he stopped them in extremely clear words from going toward non-Israelites. He stated that he had come only in search of the lost sheep of the Children of Israel. A non-Israelite woman sought a prayer for healing from him, and in response he said only this: "It is not meet to take the children's bread, and to cast it to dogs." In the parable of the banquet in the Gospel, there is also a hint toward this reality. The 'Ma'rufat' (recognized truths) upon which your call was based could serve as proof and authority for the Children of Israel, but it was not possible for other nations to understand them. For this reason, this call was entirely unsuitable for other nations due to its very nature. Consequently, the fact is that other nations before whom this call was presented did not understand it at all. They only understood from the Gospels that Jesus performed countless miracles. The effect this had on them was that on the strength of these miracles, they turned him into a deity. 

Omission of Unnecessary Parts in Describing Stories: 'anni qad ji'tukum bi-ayatim-mir-rabbikum' (that I have come to you with a sign from your Lord). Here, after 'Rasulan ila bani Isra'il', that entire part of Sayyidina Jesus's story is omitted which relates to the duration between this glad tiding and his practically rising before the Children of Israel with the call of Messengership. The Quran has used this method of omission much in describing the stories of the prophets (PBUT). Its benefit is that the reader's attention remains focused on the original objective of the speech; no redundant thing is allowed to interfere in between. Here too, the same is the case. After clarifying his purpose of advent, he was effectively stood before the Children of Israel as a caller: that he gave them this call and showed these signs as proof of his Messengership. 

A Difference between the Description of the Torah and Quran: Among the miracles mentioned here, all except the first and the last are also mentioned in the Gospel. However, in the Quran, the qualifier "bi-idhnillah" (by Allah's leave) is attached to each of them, but such clarifications are missing from the Gospel. This is because when the concept of divinity for Sayyidina Jesus (PBUH) was produced, such words would have been removed as being inconsistent with the concept of divinity. But where could these people hide the realities? Despite all their efforts, such testimonies of pure Tawheed exist in the Gospels today that one wonders how the Christians fell into Shirk despite these bright evidences. We will point toward some things later. 'ji'tukum bi-ayah': the indefiniteness of the word 'Ayah' expresses generalization rather than unity—meaning I have come with a sign from my Lord as proof of my Messengership, regardless of what their number is.

Two meanings of 'Musaddiqan lima bayna yadayhi': 'Musaddiqan' is a state (Hal), but it is conjoined to the previous sentence merely due to similarity. It has two meanings, and we clarified both meanings elsewhere. One is: I confirm the Torah. Evidences of this confirmation exist in the Gospels. Jesus stated with great force and emphasis repeatedly that he had not come to abrogate the Torah but to establish it; he also stated that heaven and earth would pass away but not one jot or tittle of the Torah would pass away until all was fulfilled. The Sharia he practically followed himself and instructed his followers to follow was the Sharia of the Torah; the addition he made to the Torah was not of Sharia but only of wisdom, and the nature of this addition is that he opened that interior of the Torah to which the eyes of the Jewish scholars and Pharisees were closed. His namesakes announced rebellion against the Torah only from the time of Paul. The second meaning is: I am the referent of the Torah's prophecies; they have been confirmed by my manifestation. Prophecies existed from the prophets preceding Sayyidina Jesus (PBUH) on the basis of which the Jews were waiting for the advent of a prophet. Consequently, when Jesus gained fame, the rumor began in many circles that the one awaited had arrived. Some people called this awaited one Elijah. It is also reported in the Gospels regarding Yahya that when he was in jail by Herod's order, he sent some of his disciples to Jesus to ask: "Art thou he that should come, or do we look for another?" Jesus told the messengers: go and tell what you see—"the lame walk... the dumb speak... the blind see... now what else is there to wait for?" Sayyidina Jesus (PBUH) himself also referenced several things that previous prophets said about him. These references exist in the Gospels. 'wa li-uhilla lakum ba'dhal-ladhi hurrima 'alaykum' (and to make lawful for you some of what was forbidden to you) is also conjoined to the meaning: making lawful some forbidden things refers to making lawful those things which the Jewish scholars had prohibited merely through their self-invented fatwas and their extremism, and these things had entered the Sharia as tradition. For example, take the issue of the respect of the Sabbath. This story had been increased by the Jewish jurists and Pharisees to such an extent that even praying for healing for a patient on the Sabbath was contrary to the respect of the Sabbath in their view; consequently, the mention of several debates between Jesus and the Jewish scholars on the issue of Sabbath respect is in the Gospels. Similarly, the mention of several such traditions is in the Gospels which Sayyidina Jesus and his disciples openly broke, and when the Jewish scholars accused them of irreligiousness upon their breaking, you (PBUH) thoroughly exposed their false religiosity.

The Reality of Using the word 'Ab' (Father) for God: The expression "my Father and your Father" that appears repeatedly in the Gospels for God—the Quran has corrected this: what Sayyidina Jesus (PBUH) actually said was that Allah is my Lord and your Lord, so worship Him. But the Christians followed 'Mutashabihat' and ignored your clear teachings. In Hebrew, the word 'Ab' is used for both 'father' and 'Lord'; similarly, the word 'Ibn' comes in the meanings of both 'son' and 'servant.' Obviously, when a word is used in two meanings, the context determines which meaning is intended; but when the Christians made the creed of Jesus's divinity, they used whatever they found useful for the support of this creed, regardless of its occasion and place. Then, when only translations remained in place of the original Gospel, the expression of everything also changed entirely into something else. But despite all these alterations, such clarifications exist in the Gospel today from which it is perfectly clear that when Jesus calls God 'Ab', his meaning is 'Lord' (Rabb). Thus, in some places, he clarified the objective by using other synonyms for this word; thus, in John 20:17: "but go to my brethren, and say unto them, I ascend unto my Father, and your Father; and to my God, and your God." From this instruction, it is perfectly clear that in the sense Jesus calls Allah Almighty his 'Ab', in that sense he calls Him the 'Ab' of all creation; and from this it is also clear that he uses this word for the expression of Allah Almighty from the aspect of His Lordship, and not to link his own lineage with Him. Furthermore, he also uses the expression 'God' for God, and just as he calls Him the God of others, he calls Him his own God too. 'hadha siratum-mustaqim': meaning, the straight path to reach God is only this: that He alone is accepted as the Lord of all—one's own and others'—and He alone is worshipped. Those who have produced other means and intermediaries in between have produced many twists and turns in this straight path, due to which they have wandered into the valleys of Shirk and misguidance. This path is without any deviation ('iwaj); it leads straight to God. The indefiniteness (nakarah) here points toward the importance and glory of this highway of nature.

Meaning of 'Hawari': The word 'Hawari' in Arabic seems to have come from Hebrew. Linguists differ in its literal meaning. In our view, its meaning is well-wisher, supporter, helper, and aid. Just as the word 'Ansar' became specified for those life-risking people of Madinah who supported the Holy Prophet (PBUH) from the beginning of the call, similarly the word 'Hawariyyun' was used for those specific disciples of Sayyidina Jesus (PBUH) who believed in him, remained with him in all kinds of mild and harsh circumstances, whom he taught and trained day and night with full compassion and care, and who finally reached every settlement of the Children of Israel as his callers, heralds, and messengers. The mention of these disciples exists in detail in the Gospel. 'Ansar' is the plural of both 'Nasir' and 'Nasir'. The meaning is clear. In our view, as we hinted above, there is no such difference in meaning between the words 'Ansar' and 'Hawariyyun'. Consequently, from the aspect of this shared meaning, the Quran has presented the 'Hawariyyun' as an example before the 'Ansar' of Madinah, as we will describe in Surah As-Saff. 

The meaning of the verse is: when Sayyidina Jesus (PBUH) felt from the attitude of the scholars and leaders of the Children of Israel that these people were not going to believe, he turned all his attention toward those poor companions of his who, although they did not possess rank or status, were blessed with the wealth of faith. It has been the Sunnah of the prophets (PBUT) that initially they tried to shake and wake the influential people of their respective nations, but when they saw that these people intoxicated with the sleep of heedlessness were not going to turn over, they left these intoxicated ones to their state and focused all their attention on their poor, believing companions. The repeated instruction in the Holy Quran to the Prophet (PBUH) to turn away from the disbelievers and remind the believers is about this very stage. And this is the stage in which Sayyidina Jesus (PBUH) addressed the fishermen by the riverside: "Follow me, and I will make you fishers of men."

An Aspect of the Prophets' Character: This verse also sheds light on the character of the prophets (PBUT): that they do not become despondent or heartbroken by the corruption of circumstances and the nation's stubbornness. Rather, they continue their struggle in the path of God; if people possessing power and influence do not support them, they start their journey taking only their poor, loyal, and weak/uninfluential companions. The darkness of circumstances produces light within them, and the nation's lack of favor produces further strength and resolve within them. (Hadi ra tez-tar mi khwan chu mahmil ra giran bini). In the exegesis of Surah Nuh, we will discuss this aspect of the prophets' character in detail, God willing.

'man ansari ilallah' (Who are my helpers for Allah?) expresses, on one hand, that zeal for calling to which we hinted above; on the other hand, it expresses their action and that resolve and determination which is entirely independent of every companionship and partnership besides Allah. If you reflect, you will realize that within this heralding of the call, the point is also hidden: "I have set out on my Lord's path—see here; now whoever has the courage, let them accompany me in this thorny valley." This decisive resolve of a prophet has the effect of running a wave of life even through the dead. The souls that possess some capability not only wake up but tremble with longing, and when they tremble, they cover the destination of years in moments. 

A Nuance of Language: For those who recognize the nuances of Arabic, one point is worth mentioning here. Sayyidina Jesus (PBUH) said 'man ansari ilallah', but the Hawariyyun responded not with 'nahnu ansaruka ilallah' (we are your helpers for Allah), but responded without hesitation: 'nahnu ansarullah' (we are the helpers of Allah). In Sayyidina Jesus's words, 'ila' clarifies that distance which lies between the path and the destination, and as a caller, it was befitting of him to inform of the difficulties of this path and the distance in between; but the Hawariyyun, in a single leap of the zeal of devotion, effectively covered the entire distance in their response, and in this delicate stage of the call to truth, this was the thing befitting their spirit of faith and Islam. It should also be noted here that while there is great brevity in Sayyidina Jesus's question, there is great detail in the Hawariyyun's response. They affirmed their faith, made Sayyidina Jesus a witness to their being Muslims, and along with the affirmation of their faith and following the Messenger, they also prayed to God to be registered among those who bear witness to the truth.

Implications of "nahnu ansarullah": From this detail, it is clarified that the Hawariyyun well-understood what it means to be among the helpers of Allah and what implications and corollaries this summary consists of. It was known that among its demands is to believe in God with a sincere heart, to follow all His commands without hesitation, to believe in what He revealed, to follow the Messenger He sent, and to bear witness through word, deed, life, and death to the truth of which God made them custodians. This is that testimony which, if given by giving one's life, attains the rank of original martyrdom (Shahadat). Also remember that what the Hawariyyun specifically made Sayyidina Jesus a witness to is their being Muslims. From this, the point emerges that in the minds of the Hawariyyun, there was only the concept of Islam and Muslim; there was no concept of Christians and Christianity. This point relates to the 'column' ('amud) of this Surah. We clarified in the introductory discussions of the Surah that the column of this Surah is Islam.

'faktubna ma'ash-shahidin' (so register us among the witnesses): this is a prayer that on the Day of Resurrection, their count be written among those who bear witness to the truth, not among those who hide the truth. This very 'Shahadat-e-Haqq' (bearing witness to the truth) is that original responsibility which is placed by Allah Almighty upon the Ummah of every prophet. The prophet bears witness to Allah's religion over the Ummah by risking his life, and after the prophet, it is the Ummah's responsibility to bear witness to this truth over the creation, being indifferent to every greed and every fear. This testimony is a demand to be given through heart, tongue, word, deed, and the sacrifice of life and wealth—every aspect. The opposite of this testimony is 'Kitman-e-Haqq' (concealment of truth), which is among the most severe crimes of the Divine Sharia. It is proven from the history of religions that the Jews became the greatest criminals of this crime, and this crime is among those for which they deserved Allah Almighty's curse. If you reflect on the interior of this prayer of the Hawariyyun, you will feel that it also contains an indirect critique of this concealment of truth by the Jews. The subject of this verse is also described with a slight change of words in Surah As-Saff: 'O you who have believed, be supporters of Allah, as Jesus, the son of Mary, said to the disciples, "Who are my supporters for Allah?" The disciples said, "We are supporters of Allah." Then a faction of the Children of Israel believed and a faction disbelieved. So We supported those who believed against their enemy, and they became dominant' (As-Saff: 14). Here, there is no need to show that Allah's help refers to the aid and support of Allah's Messenger and Allah's religion, to establish which Allah's Messenger rises. The words 'man ansari ilallah' themselves express this reality. Despite being independent of every kind of aid, Allah Almighty described this as His aid because this work is liked by Allah and in it is the welfare and well-being of His servants.

Original Meaning of the word 'Makr': 'Makr' means to make a hidden plan to harm someone. The aspect of reproach in it was produced from the fact that the use of hidden plans is proof of a person's weakness. Since the situation is generally that weak people use secret plans, the aspect of its reproach became dominant in minds and it began to be presumed that 'Makr' is necessarily reprehensible. But this point is against reality. A hidden plan must be used in some circumstances as a counter to a plotter's plan or as their punishment. If an open retaliatory action is taken against a secret plotter, they will declare it oppression and excess, and those unaware of the circumstances will consider them justified. Similarly, a hidden plan must sometimes be adopted against a conspiratorial enemy to warn them, so that it becomes manifest to them that their conspiracies are not hidden—those for whom they are weaving this net are aware of their net. This thing also humiliates them and helps in restraining them from such acts in the future, provided they possess the capability to take heed. The 'Makr' attributed to Allah Almighty here refers to this very 'Makr' which Allah Almighty adopts for the counter of the truth's enemies' conspiracies or for their crushing. These plans are so 'arrow-to-target' (effective) that the enemies are left speechless, and simultaneously countless blessings are obtained from them for the creation. 'wallahu khayrul-makirin' (and Allah is the best of planners) points toward this reality. As for the question: what plan did Allah Almighty adopt to save Sayyidina Jesus from the evil of the Jews? The suitable place for its answer will come in Surah An-Nisa.

A Shared Reality of the Prophets' Lives: The point hinted at in this verse is a shared reality of the lives of all prophets (PBUT). The lives of all prophets bear witness that when they have despaired of the dignitaries and leaders of their nation and focused their entire attention on their poor companions and the common people of the nation, and their call begins to spread its effects among these people, then these dignitaries and leaders, considering this thing a severe threat to their power, become engaged in various types of conspiracies against the prophet so as to find some pretext for his killing and get rid of this trouble according to their thought. This stage of trial has occurred in the life of every prophet, as we hinted above, but we mention here some conspiracies of the Jewish dignitaries and leaders regarding Sayyidina Jesus. It is known from the Gospel that the Jewish scholars, priests, and jurists spread various types of nets against him (PBUH) at this occasion. First, they accused him and his companions of breaking the traditions of Islam and insulting and belittling the elders so that the emotions of the masses could be incited against them. The second net they spread was to send their specific men repeatedly to ask him such questions whose answers could provide material for a fatwa of Kufr and apostasy against him. This work was performed with great activity by the Jewish jurists and Pharisees, and from within Sayyidina Jesus's parables and similes, they provided, in their own estimation, that material on the basis of which a fatwa for his execution being mandatory could be given. Third, since at that time the political power in the country was with the Romans, an attempt was made to provide material to incite them. First, questions were asked of Sayyidina Jesus (PBUH) regarding the payment of tribute so that it could be proven that this person stops people from giving tribute to Caesar. But Sayyidina Jesus gave such crushing answers to these types of questions that the Jewish scholars were left speechless. Then they accused him of claiming to be the King of Israel. For this, an attempt was made to obtain material from some of Sayyidina Jesus's parabolic statements and to incite the Roman government through it. The fourth plan made was that from among the twelve disciples of Sayyidina Jesus, one disciple, Judas—who was a hypocrite—was bribed by the Jews to agree to inform on him (PBUH) and have him arrested. The detail of all these conspiracies exists in the Gospels. If there were no fear of lengthiness, we would gather all this material here in a suitable order, but it seemed better that just as the Quran sufficed with a hint, we too suffice with hints. 

This is that very stage of a prophet's life in which he leaves the nation and, announcing disavowal of his enemies, migrates; and this migration manifests in various forms, the detail of which will come in its place.

Allah's Plan for the Protection of Sayyidina Jesus: Now the best hidden plan is described which Allah Almighty adopted to save Sayyidina Jesus (PBUH) from the Jews' conspiracy and by which all the threads of their conspiracy were scattered. "Tawaffi" in the original Arabic lexicon means 'al-akhdhu bit-tamam'—to take something in full or to seize something toward oneself. The use of this word in the meaning of giving death is not literal but metaphorical. Such words used in both their literal and metaphorical meanings are in need of context for determining their correct objective. Here, the following contexts are against taking its meaning as giving death. First, the occasion is of glad tiding and a promise of victory from Allah Almighty for Sayyidina Jesus and his companions. The stories of all messengers are witnesses that when their nations intended their killing, Allah Almighty gave them the glad tiding of His protection and victory. Here too, look at the verse and it will be known that the entire verse is of glad tiding and a promise of victory. In this context, after all, what is the place for saying "I am going to give you death"? That would be the very thing the Jews desired. The difference would only be in the medium: that death occurred not at the hands of the Jews but at the hands of Providence. Second, if giving death is intended by this word here, then the words 'rafi'uka ilayya' (raising you to Me) after it remain entirely unnecessary. After all, what is the benefit in saying "I am going to give you death and raise you toward Me"? The context proves that here, after 'mutawaffika', the words 'wa rafi'uka ilayya' are clarifying the form of the 'Tawaffi': that the form of your 'Tawaffi' will be that I will raise you toward Me. Third, it is not correct to take the meaning of 'wa rafi'uka ilayya' as merely elevating ranks. In this case, the word 'ilayya' (to Me) remains entirely without need, and no word in the Quran is used without need. If only the elevation of rank were intended, then according to Arabic grammar, 'rafi'uka' was sufficient; 'ilayya' was not needed. Look in the Quran: wherever this word is used for the subject of elevation of status, it is used without 'ila'. For example: '...of them were those to whom Allah spoke, and He raised [rafa'a] some of them in degree' (Al-Baqarah: 253). 'And if We had willed, We could have elevated [rafa'nahu] him thereby, but he adhered [instead] to the earth...' (Al-A'raf: 176). 'wa rafa'nahu makanan 'aliyya' (And We raised him to a high station) (Maryam: 57). If the true right of the particle 'ila' is fulfilled—and it is necessary to fulfill this right—then the meaning of 'wa rafi'uka ilayya' will be: "I am going to raise you toward Me with honor and respect." Fourth, where the Quran has described this subject in another place, it has completely removed the word 'mutawaffika'; after the negation of killing and the cross, the thing affirmed is only the being raised. "bal rafa'ahullahu ilayh: rather, Allah raised him to Himself." This is an extremely clear context that the Quran has told the original form of "Tawaffi": that Allah Almighty raised him toward Himself. Observe the verse: 'And [for] their saying, "Indeed, we have killed the Messiah, Jesus, the son of Mary, the messenger of Allah." And they did not kill him, nor did they crucify him; but [another] was made to resemble him to them. And indeed, those who differ over it are in doubt about it. They have no knowledge of it except the following of assumption. And they did not kill him, for certain. Rather, Allah raised him to Himself. And ever is Allah Exalted in Might and Wise' (An-Nisa: 157-158). This verse held the most suitable place for describing how Sayyidina Jesus died. This is because here the Quran has refuted with great emphasis and intensity those who claimed his killing or his cross. If your death had occurred, at this occasion the Quran would have clearly said: he was not killed nor was he crucified, but Allah gave him death. But the Quran not only did not say this, but it did not even use the word 'Tawaffi'; it only used the words 'rafa'ahullahu ilayh'. Every person of taste can estimate to what extent there is room for taking death as the meaning from this 'raising' after the negation of killing and the cross.

'wa mutahhiruka minal-ladhina kafaru': meaning, I will separate you from this filthy society and include you in the category of the righteous and good. The Sunnah of Allah for the prophets (PBUT) is that they reside within the nation they are sent to reform as long as there is some expectation of them believing. This expectation ends when the people of the nation pursue the killing of the prophet. At that time, the prophet migrates by Divine command. Then, just as after the separation of the soul no form remains for the body except rotting and decaying, similarly after the separation of the prophet no path remains for those who belied him except defeat and disgrace. The prophet and his companions emerge from the filthy environment and enter a pure and healthy environment, which increases their spiritual power and health. In contrast, the enemies of the prophet are entirely deprived of life-giving elements and set out with full speed toward the valley of destruction. The teacher-imam has discussed these effects and results of migration in detail in the exegesis of Surah Al-Kafirun. This celestial raising of Sayyidina Jesus is also a type of migration; therefore, just as all messengers received glad tidings of victory and success after migration, he too received the glad tiding of success and dominance along with this migration, as described next.

'wa ja'ilu-l-ladhina-ttaba'uka fawqal-ladhina kafaru...': This contains the glad tiding that the name-bearers of the Messiah will always be dominant over his deniers. Historically, it is a factual matter that the Christians as a nation have always been dominant and overbearing over the Jews since this glad tiding. Even today, when apparently a state of the Jews has been established in a small region, this reality remains established and proven in its place just as it was before. This is because this so-called state of the Jews was established at the hands of the Christians and exists on their strength.

Removal of a Doubt: One point certainly troubles the heart here: when are these Christians themselves followers of the Messiah? They are entirely innovators and entirely deviated from the Messiah's teaching. The answer in our view is that 'alladhina-ttaba'uka' (those who followed you) here refers not only to their correct type of followers but includes their general followers and name-bearers too. Several points go in favor of this opinion of ours. For example, the words 'Ahl al-Kitab' and 'Alladhina utu al-Kitab' have also been used in two different meanings in the Quran. In some places, the People of the Book are intended by them as a group, regardless of what their actual beliefs and deeds are; and in some places, only the true People of the Book are intended. Similarly, in our view, 'alladhina-ttaba'uka' holds a broad meaning. All those affiliated with Sayyidina Jesus are included in it, whether they are his true followers or merely name-bearers. Second, here 'alladhina-ttaba'uka' is placed opposite 'alladhina kafaru' (those who disbelieved), from which the context emerges that the contrast is in reality between the deniers of the Messiah and those affiliated with the Messiah, not between the sincere and the innovators. Third, the occasion is of glad tiding. The demand of glad tiding is that it be broad. If 'alladhina-ttaba'uka' referred only to true followers, the circle of the glad tiding would remain very limited. Just as when Allah Almighty gave the glad tiding of provision for the descendants of Abraham, He did not limit it only to the believers but kept it general for both believers and non-believers; similarly here, 'alladhina-ttaba'uka' is general for pure and non-pure followers.

A Messenger is a Court for his Nation: We hinted above that among the prophets, those who are exalted to the rank of Messenger (Rasul) hold the status of a court for their nation. Through them, the decision between truth and falsehood for the nation is necessarily made. The Messenger and his companions attain dominance, and his opponents suffer defeat. Regardless of whether this dominance is achieved in the presence of the Messenger or after his departure. In the light of the Quran's clarification regarding Sayyidina Jesus, it has been known above that he was not merely a prophet but 'rasulan ila bani Isra'il' (a Messenger to the Children of Israel). It was a mandatory demand of this position of his that his followers obtain that dominance over his opponents of which the glad tiding is in this verse. The verse "I and My Messengers shall surely prevail" also contains the statement of this Sunnah of Allah. This is that very court whose mention appears repeatedly in the Gospels. Due to this distinguishing characteristic of the Messengers, Allah Almighty does not give their enemies the respite to kill them. Consequently, the killing of any of the Messengers is not proven. This point also goes against the Christians' claim that Sayyidina Jesus was crucified. Detailed discussion on this issue will come in Surah Al-Ma'idah.

This is the manifestation of that 'court' to which we hinted above and which is the mandatory result of a Messenger's advent to a nation. It contains the threat of both worldly punishment and the punishment of the Hereafter. The heart-shaking calamities that came upon the Jews in this world were all the result of this Kufr of theirs.

'wallahu la yuhibbu-zh-zhalimin' (And Allah does not love the wrongdoers) also yields the hint that those claiming to believe in the Messiah will also not be able to escape the grasp of the Hereafter if they fall into Shirk and Bid'ah after faith, because Allah Almighty does not like those who fall into Shirk and Bid'ah after faith and thus become wrongdoers against their own souls.

Digression to the Prophet: This verse and the five verses alongside it hold the nature of a digression (Iltifat) toward the Prophet (PBUH). In the middle of the speech, addressing you, consolation was given against the attitude of the opponents, especially the Christians, and some necessary instructions were also given. The meaning is: this entire history of Jesus that has been related to you—this is the original reality of the Messiah. This is not the type of self-invented story that the Christians have authored; rather, these are Allah's verses and this is a wise reminder. Meaning: the Christians have turned it into a mythology from which only misguidance can be obtained, but Allah has revealed it anew through you so that the paths of true guidance and wisdom and admonition open. Exactly this type of digression is coming ahead in verse 108. From that, the clarification of some words of this verse also occurs. It is stated: 'These are the verses of Allah We recite to you in truth, and Allah wants no injustice to the worlds' (Al Imran: 108). Meaning: He clarified the truth anew so that no excuse remains for people to persist in misguidance. And if they persist in misguidance, the responsibility is their own.

The Example of Jesus (PBUH) is that of Adam (PBUH): This verse is the concluding verse of the discussion in this chapter. The meaning is: just as God created Adam from dust and said to him "Be" and he was, similarly Allah Almighty created Jesus (PBUH) from His word 'Kun'. In fact, in the matter of birth, Adam holds preference over Jesus (PBUH) in the sense that neither a father nor a mother had a role in his birth; so when the Christians do not accept him as a deity, why then did they make Sayyidina Jesus (PBUH) a deity?

Usage of the word 'Ibn' (Son) for others: Just as there was no reason for any misconception from the matter of birth, similarly from the word 'Ibn', if the Christians had used their reason, there was no reason to fall into any misguidance. In the Torah and Gospel, the word 'Ibn' is used not only for Jesus (PBUH) but also for Sayyidina Adam (see Luke 3:38). It is also used for the angels (see Genesis 6:2, 4). It is also used for Sayyidina Jacob (PBUH) (see Deuteronomy 14:1). It is also used for the Christians (see John 1:11-12). If this word is enough to make someone a deity, then there remains no specificity for Sayyidina Jesus (PBUH); then an entire army of deities can be prepared. Why did the Christians suffice only with Sayyidina Jesus (PBUH)?

Effectively, the discussion has reached the final point of the finality of proof (itmam al-hujjah); for this reason, the Quran closed the door of further debate and discussion by saying 'al-haqqu mir-rabbika...' (The truth is from your Lord...). In this sentence, in our view, the subject (mubtada') is omitted, and we clarified elsewhere that when the subject is omitted, the purpose is to focus the addressee's entire attention on the predicate (khabar). Meaning: the original reality regarding Sayyidina Jesus is this which the Quran has clarified; whatever else exists is all the myth-making of the Christians. In 'fala takum-minal-mumtarin' (so do not be among the doubters), the apparent address is to the Prophet (PBUH), but in such instances, as we clarified in more than one place, the focus of the speech is not toward the Prophet but toward the Ummah; and if some rebuke is hidden within it, its connection is in reality with the opponents. But they no longer remain worthy of address; therefore, the point is said addressing one's own people instead of them.

We have discussed the word "Al-'Ilm" elsewhere: in the Quran, it refers to that true knowledge which comes from Allah Almighty through revelation. Its opposite word is "Zann" (assumption/conjecture). 

Some Omissions in the Verse: In this verse, according to the style of the Arabic language, some things are omitted. If the omissions were made explicit, the full point would be: 'nad'u nahnu abna'ana wa antum abna'akum wa nuhdhiru nahnu wa anfusana wa antum wa anfusakum thumma nabtahil nahnu wa antum'. We have unfolded these omissions in our translation. 

Occasion and Place of Mubahala: "Ibtihal" means prayer and supplication, but it also contains the meaning of 'abandonment'; for this reason, it is well-known for the prayer of mutual cursing. In matters where the basis of difference is something rational and argumentative, the correct method for settling the issue is reason and argument; but where all stages of reason and argument have been crossed, the addressee is entirely devoid of proof and authority, the truth is as bright as the sun before them, and there is no path of escape or avoidance for them, yet they persist in their point merely to maintain the 'honor' of their stubbornness and obstinacy—then for such occasions, the method of 'Mubahala' holds the status of the final remedy. History proves that the Christians did not have the audacity to accept this challenge of the Quran, which clarified to the final degree that they did not consider their stance regarding Sayyidina Jesus to be correct but supported it merely due to their communal prejudice. In contrast, this open challenge from the Holy Prophet (PBUH) is an extremely open proof that he was perfectly certain of the correctness and truthfulness of his stance. The inclusion of his own family, kith, and kin with him in the Mubahala increases its seriousness and importance twofold—rather, tenfold—because no person would dare to knowingly curse their own wife, children, and loved ones.

Meaning: the original reality of Sayyidina Jesus is this which was described. Whatever rank and status he has is in the capacity of a servant of God and His Prophet and Messenger. He has no share in God's divinity. The Deity is only Allah Almighty, and He is the Mighty and Wise. 'Aziz' (Mighty), meaning dominant over all and superior to all; 'Hakim' (Wise), meaning His every action is based on wisdom and benefit—these two attributes completely negate Shirk.

In this verse, it was stated that Mubahala was the final form for settling this dispute, but if they are not pleased even with that, its clear meaning is that they do not want to follow the truth but want to create corruption in God's earth by opposing the truth, because Shirk is the root of all corruption. If there were many deities in the heaven and earth, their entire creative system would be in disarray; similarly, if some room for Shirk is accepted in religion, the entire system of justice and equity of this world would be in disarray.

Subject of Verses 64 to 71: After clarifying the original reality of Sayyidina Jesus (PBUH) and completing the proof over the Christians, both Jews and Christians are addressed and invited to Tawheed and Islam. It begins by declaring Tawheed a shared reality: just as Islam has brought its call, previous prophets and scriptures also gave the call to this very thing; therefore, if you belie Tawheed, you do not belie only the Quran but also your own prophets and your own scriptures. Then, Prophet Abraham (PBUH) is referenced: why do you taint his name in support of your innovations? He was neither a Jew nor a Christian; he was a Hanif Muslim. The Torah and Gospel were revealed after him, and you set up the offshoots of Judaism and Christianity after them; then why do you try to drag him into your support? The ones deserving of affiliation and proximity with him can only be those who follow his religion of Islam, and if this honor is attained, it is attained by Muhammad (PBUH) and those who believe in him, not you who are at the forefront of opposing Islam. Following this, the Muslims are warned: guard yourselves against the Fitnas of these People of the Book. Their entire effort is for this: to turn you away from the straight path and put you on the path of misguidance. Simultaneously, the People of the Book are also rebuked: knowing well what the truth is, to oppose this truth and try to turn others away from this truth too—after all, what is this profession you have chosen for yourselves while being People of the Book? In this light, recite the following verses.

Although the address "Ya Ahl al-Kitab" (O People of the Book) is equal for both Jews and Christians, the Christians are specifically addressed in this Surah; therefore, the focus of the speech is more toward them. 

Linguistic Research of the word 'Sawa'': 'Sawa'' means middle. 'Sawa' ar-ra's' (the middle of the head). Something that is exactly in the middle of two will be equally shared, recognized, and known by both. Regarding Tawheed, the Quran's claim is that it is equally shared and recognized between the People of the Book and the Muslims. The Quran began the discussion with them by making this shared word the foundation: when Tawheed is a shared reality between us and you, then perform a comparison as to whether the Quran and Islam stand up to the standard of this common denominator, or Judaism and Christianity? This method of debate is perfectly in accord with that established method which the Quran instructed in the verse: 'Invite to the way of your Lord with wisdom and good instruction...' (An-Nahl: 125). A specific aspect of this method of wisdom is that if a shared foundation can be found for discussion with the addressee, then the conversation should be advanced upon it, without unnecessarily trying to impose one's own individuality. Consequently, the Quran adopted this method here. The People of the Book, by virtue of being bearers of heavenly scriptures, were well-acquainted with the teaching of Tawheed and were also claimants of being its champions. In their scriptures, the teaching of Tawheed existed in extremely clear words. If they had adopted Shirk, they did not do so because there was any room for Shirk in their religion, but entirely contrary to the teachings of their prophets and scriptures, they adopted this thing merely through the path of innovation and then, by following 'Mutashabihat' (as we hinted above), tried to invent various arguments in its favor. The Quran invited them: this point is equally recognized between us and you—that none except Allah be worshipped, no partner be appointed to Him, and none of us take one another as lords; then, contrary to this recognized and shared reality, why have you made others partners in the worship of God and given your priests, monks, jurists, and Sufis the status of 'arbabam-min-dunillah' (lords besides Allah)? He began the debate from this point and then gradually clarified its requirements and corollaries and refuted the things that had arisen in the People of the Book contrary to its requirements. The point that Tawheed fundamentally holds the status of a shared reality between us and the People of the Book does not require explanation. Anyone who keeps an eye on the Torah and Gospel is well-acquainted with it. As far as the Torah is concerned, the teaching of Tawheed is in it with such clarity, certainty, and frequency that quoting its references would merely lengthen the point. However, we present some references from the Gospel here because the greatest misguidance regarding Tawheed has occurred to the Christians. And in the verse, in reality—as we hinted above—the focus of the speech is indeed toward them.

Evidences of Tawheed in the Gospels: In Luke 4:8: "And Jesus answered and said unto him, Get thee behind me, Satan: for it is written, Thou shalt worship the Lord thy God, and him only shalt thou serve." In Mark 12:29-30: "And Jesus answered him, The first of all the commandments is, Hear, O Israel; The Lord our God is one Lord." In John 17:3: "And this is life eternal, that they might know thee the only true God, and Jesus Christ, whom thou hast sent." In Matthew 19:17: "And he said unto him, Why callest thou me good? there is none good but one, that is, God." Here, the word translated as "good" (neki) should, in our view, be translated as "pure" (paki). Thus, "there is none good but one" is also not a correct translation. This will originally be "there is none pure but one." The translation of this segment of the Gospel is different in some other versions. Although this too is wrong, it has relative clarity. Observe: "Why do you call me good? None is good except one, and that is Allah." This sentence too is originally: "Why do you declare me pure? None is pure except one, and that is Allah."

In the presence of these clear teachings of Tawheed, how rational is the Quran's demand of the People of the Book that they too examine their creeds in the light of these texts and purify their creeds from the things they included in them entirely contrary to these, merely by following innovations and 'Mutashabihat.' Then finally, He instructed the Muslims: if these people turn away from the teachings of their own prophets and scriptures, then you clarify that we are not ones to turn away from these realities; we surrender ourselves to that same One Lord, and this is in reality original Islam. In this verse, the point that appeared—"none of us take one another as lords"—its clarification occurred in another place: that the People of the Book, contrary to this instruction, made their priests and monks lords. Upon this, a question was asked of the Holy Prophet (PBUH) by some of the People of the Book: "We do not accept the priests and monks as lords"? The Prophet replied: "Is it not the case that whatever they declare Haram, you declare it Haram, and whatever they declare Halal, you declare it Halal?" The inquirer admitted that this was the case. You (PBUH) said: this is making them lords. And when someone is obeyed in such a way that the right of prohibition and permission is recognized for them, then in reality this thing is synonymous with worshipping them, regardless of whether prostration and bowing are apparently performed to them or not. At the end of the verse, this point was also clarified: if these People of the Book are not ready to accept even this shared reality of Tawheed, then you clearly announce to them: bear witness that we are Muslims. These words "bear witness" are by way of expressing disavowal. Meaning: listen and be witnesses to the fact that we delivered the message to you with full clarity. Now tomorrow before God, the entire responsibility is yours. We have fulfilled our duty. Through 'bi-anna muslimun' (that we are Muslims), a hint is made toward the reality that this very Tawheed is the soul of that entrustment and surrender of which Islam consists and which is the original sought-after goal. Whoever does not possess this Tawheed does not possess Islam, and whoever does not possess Islam does not possess God.

Exegesis of Verses 65 to 68: There is no grammatical or literary difficulty in these verses. Their subject has also been described in full detail in the exegesis of Surah Al-Baqarah. Since Prophet Abraham (PBUH) was the recognized ancestral and spiritual leader of the Children of Israel and the Ishmaelites alike, all three groups—Jews, Christians, and Arab polytheists—tried to use his name in support of their respective innovations. The Jews said Prophet Abraham was on our path. The Christians described him as being on their path, and the Arab polytheists on theirs. Although this claim of pride always existed among each of these groups against the others, after the start of Islam's call, the specific weapon all three groups used in its opposition was this: that the new religion is against the Abrahamic religion; we are the bearers of the original Abrahamic religion, and Muhammad (PBUH) wants to mislead us by turning us away from our original ancestral religion. The Quran refuted their propaganda here: the revelation of the Torah and Gospel occurred centuries after Prophet Abraham; then how was he upon Judaism or Christianity? Even for a foolish statement, there is usually some small foundation. You have produced disputes even in some matters about which you had some knowledge; for those, you can lean on some justification and console yourselves; but this statement of yours is entirely 'foot-in-the-air' (baseless); after all, in a matter regarding which you have no information at all, what room is there for justification to interfere in rational matters? What kind of madness of opposition and enmity for the truth is this, that even such a simple point does not enter your understanding!

Following this, the Quran stated Prophet Abraham's (PBUH) religion: that he was neither a Jew nor a Christian but a Hanif Muslim. "Hanif" means, as clarified in Surah Al-Baqarah, 'single-minded'—meaning he was on the straight path of Tawheed. He had not adopted the twisted polytheistic paths apart from it, and he was a 'Muslim'—meaning obedient to his Lord. From this, the point emerged that Judaism and Christianity are twisted paths deviated from Tawheed which lead toward misguidance instead of guidance. Along with this, the clarification was also made that just as Prophet Abraham (PBUH) has no connection to Judaism and Christianity, he has no connection to the polytheists either. This point was said by separating it from the general context of the sentence because it is in refutation of the polytheists of the Ishmaelites, who are not directly addressed in this Surah. The address of this Surah, as clarified above, is to the People of the Book, especially the Christians; if any point in refutation of the polytheists has appeared in it, it has appeared only incidentally. This point too is among the incidental points, and the need for its mention, as we hinted above, was because just as the Jews and Christians presented Prophet Abraham's (PBUH) name in support of their misguidances, similarly—rather, with even more zeal and clamor—the polytheists of the Quraysh presented his name in their support; in fact, their claim was that the religion they are on, they received as an inheritance from Prophet Abraham. After this, it was stated that the true rightful claimants of affiliation with Abraham are those who followed him. Meaning: this affiliation is not something obtained only from family and lineage, but it relates to following and obedience. In this regard, the most prior and closest to Prophet Abraham are this Prophet (Muhammad PBUH) and the companions who believe in him, and not the Jews, Christians, and polytheists who have completely distorted and ruined the Abrahamic religion. Finally, it stated: these are the believers whose ally is Allah; He will help them and grant them dominance over their opponents because they alone are upon that true religion which Prophet Abraham brought.

Exegesis of Verses 69 to 70: The first of these verses is an address to the Muslims as a warning: the Jews and Christians well know that Prophet Abraham had no minor connection at all to these innovations of Judaism and Christianity, but despite this, they are doing propaganda solely to turn you away from your true religion, even though through this effort they are only arranging for their own deprivation and misguidance. One who deliberately tries to turn another away from the path of truth to prove their own misguidance as guidance, first of all, plunges themselves into misguidance. But in the zeal of opposing another, they do not feel the original result of their action. The subsequent two verses address the People of the Book, and in both, the repetition of "Ya Ahl al-Kitab" expresses sorrow and reproach: it is regrettable that being People of the Book, you chose the profession of misleading instead of guiding, and the profession of concealing truth instead of expressing truth.

'wa antum tash-hadun' can have two meanings. One, that today you are denying Allah's verses while your hearts are bearing witness regarding them that these are Allah's verses. Second, that today, in the zeal of envy and enmity, you are spending your entire force to belie the truth whose aid, confirmation, and the duty of bearing witness to which before the creation a covenant has already been taken from you and you have acknowledged the carrying of this responsibility. The first subject requires no proof. For this second meaning, a parallel exists further on in this Surah. It was stated: 'And [remember, O Children of Israel], when Allah took the covenant of the prophets, [saying], "Whatever I give you of the Scripture and wisdom and then there comes to you a messenger confirming what is with you, you [must] believe in him and support him." [Allah] said, "Have you acknowledged and taken upon that My commitment?" They said, "We have acknowledged it." He said, "Then bear witness, and I am with you among the witnesses."' (Al Imran: 81). The full explanation of this verse is coming ahead.

The clarification of mixing truth and falsehood with each other has occurred well in the exegesis of Surah Al-Baqarah. The Jews had distorted the entire Torah with their alterations, making the distinction between truth and falsehood difficult; but here, the hint is specifically toward those alterations they made within the circumstances and events related to Prophet Abraham, Prophet Ishmael, and the construction of Baitullah, and within the prophecies of the Holy Prophet (PBUH). The purpose of these alterations was to sever Prophet Abraham's connection to Makkah and Baitullah in such a way that the truths described by the prophets regarding the Holy Prophet (PBUH) could be veiled. From the Quran's words "wa antum ta'lamun" (while you know), it is known that the Jewish scholars of the era of the Quran's revelation were also aware of these alterations, and in reality, the nature of these alterations is such that they can be seized upon with minor reflection. It should be noted that the character of the Jewish masses is not under discussion here, but the character of their scholars. The context and the words of the verse are proof of this. 

Subject of Verses 72-76: Some Mischiefs of the Jews: Further on, some conspiracies and mischiefs of the People of the Book, especially the Jews, are mentioned, the purpose of which was to somehow turn the Muslims away from their religion. Then, the deep malice and envy within the Children of Israel against the Ishmaelites was revealed, due to which they were not ready in any way to tolerate that the Ishmaelites too be considered bearers of the Book and Sharia like them and become witnesses to their crimes before Allah. As if in this zeal of enmity, they had become the monopolists of God's favor, to grant a share in it to whom they willed and deprive whom they willed. This enmity and envy had molded the collective character and conduct of the Children of Israel against the Ishmaelites into a specific mold. They did not believe in the obligation of any moral or Shari code regarding them. They considered it a rewarding act to betray the trusts placed by them, saying: "This is the wealth of a disbeliever; there is no harm in suppressing it." The Quran referenced these points to warn the Muslims: do not expect from those whose envy and malice against you has reached this extent that any advice of theirs can be well-wishing for you and that any true word in your favor can emerge from their tongue. These people can steal even a single penny of yours; then how do you expect from them that they will fulfill your trust of a hundred thousand and bear witness to that truth regarding your Prophet of which they were made custodians? Where will those who are treacherous in the most insignificant things of the world bring the heart and gut to fulfill such a great trust! But even while describing the character of a base nation like the Jews, the Quran did not abandon the mantle of justice; rather, it praised the goodness of character in those among them who were of good character—in fact, it mentioned them first so that they might be encouraged and try to advance further in this field. These were the people who later were blessed with the bounty of Islam.

'wa yaquluna 'alallahil-kadhiba wa hum ya'lamun' (and they say about Allah the lie while they know): this refuted their self-invented and home-made fatwa mentioned above—that in dealing with the Ummiyyin, they were slandering morality and Sharia, and they themselves were aware of its being contrary to Sharia, but they had invented such pretexts merely in following their desires and greed for the world. Later, these same fatwas entered the Torah through the path of alteration. So much so that now if someone reads the Torah, they feel even in general moral and human rights and transactions that the Sharia is one thing for the Children of Israel and another for non-Israelites, who are described in the Torah as strangers and foreigners.

In this verse and in all verses of this style, the answer to the condition (jawab al-shart) is omitted. We presented some examples of this in Surah Al-Baqarah. Here, if the answer to the condition were made explicit, the full point would be: "Yes, those who fulfill Allah's covenant and guard God's limits—then those people are the pious, and Allah loves the pious."

Correction (Istidrak) of the points above: This verse holds the status of a correction to the above points of the Jews. The meaning is: the Jews' thought is entirely wrong that there is some special rank and station for them with God due to which they are superior to others and absolved of responsibilities regarding the Ummiyyin. The reality is that whatever rank and station exists with Allah is for those who fulfill the covenant made with God and monitor the limits established under that covenant in all kinds of circumstances. Those whose way is this are the pious in the sight of Allah, and Allah loves such pious servants. Those who are bold in breaking God's covenant and His limits yet claim piety and being beloved of God are merely cooking imaginary pulao. 

Generally, translators of the Quran have translated 'awfa bi-'ahdihi' as "fulfills their covenant"; in my view, the referent of the pronoun is Allah. Quranic parallels support this. Observe the next verse. Ibn Jarir has also taken this interpretation.

Subject of Verses 77 to 80: In the following verses, first there is a rebuke for the People of the Book's covenant-breaking—rather, covenant-selling—mentioned above. It stated: Allah blessed them with His speech and message, arranged for their education and purification, and honored them with His look of kindness and favor, but they sold Allah's covenant in exchange for the insignificant interests of the world and ruthlessly devalued His infinite favors; therefore, no share remains for them in the Hereafter now. Then, an indication is made toward some of their efforts of alteration: that the Book Allah granted them for guidance and direction—they performed disposals in it by twisting and turning for the purpose that what was not of Allah's Book be considered of the Book. Then, the People of the Book, especially the Christians, are invited to reflect in the light of sound reason: how could they attribute today those points to the Messiah which he, while being a bearer of the Book, Wisdom, and Prophethood, could never have said. In this light, recite the following verses. It is stated:

The research of the word "Ishtira'" (selling/buying/exchanging) passed in the exegesis of Surah Al-Baqarah. When the exchange is of a thing for a thing—which was generally the custom in ancient times—then every object can be both the 'sold item' (mabi') and the 'price' (thaman). For this reason, the 'Ishtira'' of something was not in reality 'buying' in the sense we use the word buying, but its meaning was 'exchange.' For this reason, the word "Ishtira'" is also used in the meaning of 'exchanging' and then, progressing from this meaning, in the meaning of 'giving preference.' "'Ahd Allah" (Allah's covenant) refers to the Book and Sharia because the status of the Book and Sharia is that of a treaty between Allah and His servants. Here, within this general meaning, there is a specific hint toward the covenant Allah Almighty took from the People of the Book regarding the final advent, and which the People of the Book had not only consigned to oblivion but had also tried to erase its traces from their books. "Ayman" refers to those general oaths and pledges upon which collective and civil life is based and from which an atmosphere of trust and good-will in social life and transactions is formed. The state of the Jews in this matter has become clear above: how they had invented Shari pretexts for betraying trusts and escaping the responsibilities of the oaths and pledges they made. In 'la yukallimuhumullahu wa la yanzhuru ilayhim', the negation of the verb is in terms of its original meaning—meaning Allah Almighty will not speak to them or look at them in the sense of the original meaning of speaking and looking. This style is common in the Arabic language; in fact, examples exist in every language. 

The meaning of the verse is: those who have made Allah's covenant and their own word and pledge such a buyable and sellable thing, and are ruthlessly sacrificing them for their worldly interests (the greatest amount of which is insignificant compared to the reward of the Hereafter)—for them, there is no share in the Hereafter. They have sold their jewels in exchange for pennies, and those who proved so unworthy in the matter of Allah's trust—Allah will neither speak to them now, nor look at them, nor purify them. Now in the Hereafter, for such wretched ones, there is nothing but a painful punishment. 

Expression of Intense Hatred for the Jews: Those who recognize the tone of words can estimate how much hatred and what intense disgust is hidden within these; but the People of the Book, especially the Jews, were deserving of this very thing due to their machinations mentioned above—especially because this was the nation whom Allah Almighty blessed with the honor of His specific speech and address through the medium of its Prophet. It was being trampled under the feet of the Pharaohs, so God cast a look of favor upon it and brought it out of that disgrace to seat it on the throne of leadership and Imamate. He revealed the Book for its purification and sent His prophets and messengers among it to refine and reform it; but this nation valued neither this address and speech nor this look of compassion and favor and this purification and cleansing for which God and His prophets made such arrangements; so what face does this nation have left for Allah to bless them again with the honor of His address on the Day of Judgment? It had closed all doors of hope upon itself. The negation of purification in this verse can have two aspects. One is that the Hereafter is not the place for purification. Its place is this world. When they wasted the opportunity here, they will not be able to obtain it in the Hereafter. Second, their crimes are not such that they would become pure from them after suffering a little punishment in the Hereafter; rather, these crimes are those that will sink them into Hell forever.

"Lawa, yalwi, layyan" means to twist, turn, wring, and entwine something. 'yalwuna alsinatahum bil-kitab' means that while uttering some words of the Divine Book, they twist their tongues in such a way that the words become something else entirely. 

A Strategy for Escaping the Divine Covenant: This is one of the strategies the People of the Book adopted to escape the responsibilities of the Divine covenant. In the exegesis of Al-Baqarah, where we discussed the question of alteration (tahrif), we stated that among the methods of alteration was the method of twisting the pronunciation of a word or sentence through the path of recitation in such a way that the original reality was completely lost. Both Jews and Christians have committed this crime. In its example, we mentioned the word "Marwah." This word appeared in the Torah in the series of Prophet Abraham's story: that at this place he was commanded to sacrifice his son. The Jews, while making many changes of the nature of addition and subtraction in the event of the sacrifice, also distorted the recitation of the word 'Marwah' to make it 'Moriah', 'Moria', 'Moreh', and who knows what else, so that instead of the famous hill of Makkah, Marwah, they could intend some place in Jerusalem by it, and thus completely sever the connection of Prophet Abraham and the event of his migration and sacrifice from Baitullah. Their objective in all this labor was that through this manipulation, the direction of the prophecies and hints could be turned away from the Ishmaelites and the Prophet of the Final Age (PBUH) within them, which were entered in the scriptures of the Torah. They performed a similar act in the recitation of the word "Bakkah," which we will discuss later. After mentioning this conspiracy, attention was drawn toward their audacity and daring: they commit this act with the objective that what is not of the Divine Book be given the label of the Divine Book, and what is not from Allah be presented in the name of Allah. It stated: this is knowingly inventing a lie against Allah, and what greater audacity can there be than inventing a lie against Allah.

"Hukm" means judgment and decision. Carrying the soul of this very meaning, it is used in the Quran from three different aspects. In some places, in the sense of mere decision, for example: '...and We were witnesses to their judgment [hukmihim]' (Al-Anbiya: 78); 'Then is it the judgment [hukm] of [the time of] ignorance they desire? But who is better than Allah in judgment for a people who are certain [in faith]?' (Al-Ma'idah: 50). In some places, it is used in the sense of the power of decision and insight, for example: 'And to Lot We gave judgment [hukman] and knowledge...' (Al-Anbiya: 74); 'And We gave him judgment [al-hukm] while yet a boy' (Maryam: 12). In some verses, it is in the meaning of order and command, for example: '...the command [al-hukm] is only for Allah, the Most High, the Grand'; '...His is the judgment [al-hukm], and to Him you will be returned' (Al-Qasas: 70). 

Reflecting on the occasion and place here reveals that this word is used in its second and third meanings. 
(This research of the word 'Hukm' is the insight of the teacher-imam).

'Rabbani' means God-worshipper and 'man of Allah.' This word in Arabic seems to have come from Hebrew; the word "Rabbi" appears much in the Torah and Gospel. Their forms are slightly different, but semantically no difference is known. The focus of this verse is specifically toward the Christians, who are the primary addressees in this Surah. The debate until now was mostly based on transmission (naql). In this verse, sound reason was addressed, and it was stated: it is a matter worth thinking about—how is it possible in any way that a human whom Allah Almighty blesses with the Book, Wisdom, and Prophethood would invite people to become his servants instead of Allah's? The meaning is: your innovations are not only entirely contrary to the Messiah's teachings, your recognized history, and the consensus creeds of the prophets, but sound reason also cannot accept their attribution to the Messiah. When Allah Almighty blesses a servant with the position of Prophethood and Messengership and grants them the Book and Wisdom, it is so they may bring people out of the servitude and slavery of others into the servitude and slavery of God, and not to try to take them away from God to make them their own servants. Its meaning would be that the one whom God sent in search of His flock became the one to mislead His flock himself. After all, what greater slander can there be against a Messenger of God than this? Following this, it was told that if a bearer of the Book and Wisdom and a prophet can give you a call, he can give the call to this: O people! Become God-worshippers and men of Allah, because if there can be any correct demand of your being readers and teachers of the Divine Book, it can only be this.

Then in this verse, it was stated that just as a prophet cannot invite people to become his own servants, similarly he cannot give the call to take angels and prophets as "lords besides Allah," because how can this call to Kufr be gathered with the call to faith? Will the one who brings the call of faith and Islam for you try to plunge you into Kufr after making you Muslims? In this final segment, the address has acquired some vastness—meaning alongside the Christians, a hint was also made toward the Quraysh, who had begun making and worshipping idols of angels and prophets too. 

Subject of Verses 81 to 91: Further on, first a reference is made to a comprehensive covenant taken from the People of the Book regarding the support and aid of the prophets—especially the final Prophet, Muhammad (PBUH)—and the People of the Book as a community had acknowledged it; but now they are avoiding its responsibilities, as detailed above.
